observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee to complete the conduct of inventory, direct the concerned City Officials to maintain and regularly update Property Cards, Subsidiary Ledgers, PPE Ledger Cards, stock cards and other subsidiary records, stop providing depreciation charges for unserviceable properties and facilitate their disposal.
observations.managementAction: The reorganization of the Inventory Committee and the initiation of physical inventory are ongoing. Additionally, the Accounting Department has started maintaining Ledger Cards and Subsidiary Records for newly acquired PPE, while the GSO has implemented digital Stock Cards and Property Cards for recent procurements. The cessation of depreciation charges on unserviceable properties has been enforced, and their disposal is in progress.
observations.reason: While significant progress has been made, full implementation is still underway. The need for thorough planning, coordination among departments, and proper reconciliation of records has required additional time, leading to the delay in full compliance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) to cause the donation of these PPE items to the Barangays, Department of Education and other national Government agencies, and thereafter, instruct the City Accountant to derecognize from the books these donated PPE items in order to fairly present the PPE account in the Financial Statements.
observations.managementAction: As of December 31, 2024, the total net book value of the PPEs exclusively used and controlled by the end-users amounted to ₱17.226 million. While the original recommendation was to donate these assets, Management has explored an alternative approach by considering the creation of a Usufruct Agreement with the end-users, which would ensure that the City retains ownership and control of the PPEs while allowing the end-users continued access.
observations.reason: The Management was hesitant to donate the PPEs as they considered the budgetary constraints faced by the end-users in managing repairs, maintenance, and operational costs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain Local Road Network (LRN) Ledger Cards and lapsing schedule for the road and its components, and disclose the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements; the City GSO to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network, maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and the City Accountant and the City GSO to secure from the City Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project.
observations.managementAction:
The LRN Ledger Cards and the lapsing schedule were prepared by the Accounting Office based on the list of road projects completed from 2013 to 2024, as provided by the City Engineer. The necessary disclosures have also been incorporated in the Notes to the Financial Statements. 

Management has committed to create an Inventory Committee to complete physical inventory of LRN.
observations.reason: Resource limitations, including insufficient manpower, have contributed to the delay in the full implementation of the audit recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure that all subsequent unused balance of funds received from national government agencies shall be returned immediately after completion of program and/or projects; and coordinate with DILG and other source agencies for the proper action to be done with dormant balance of fund transfers totaling ₱10.002 million.
observations.managementAction: The Management committed to coordinating with the Source Agency regarding the proper disposition of the funds and evaluating the possibility of transferring the funds to the General Fund should it be determined that the funds do not accrue to the Source Agency.
observations.reason: The delay is due to the difficulty in gathering the necessary information and documents to establish whether these funds belong to the General Fund or should accrue to the Source Agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer’s Office prepare the required monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms supported by the individual RAAF of each accountable officers and submit the same to the office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: The Management has submitted CRAAF for January to December 2023 for some Accountable Forms.
observations.reason: The delay in full compliance was due to the manual reconstruction of the CRAAF and Individual RAAF. This was necessary after the original files, saved on an external drive, were corrupted between May and June 2023.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Inventory Committee undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year,  prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI),  reconcile said report with the records of the City Accounting Office; direct the City Accountant to use moving average method as costing method for valuation of inventories; instruct the CGSO and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards; the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly SSMIs to the City Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office is in the process of completing the Stock Ledger Cards, while the GSO has begun maintaining digital Stock Cards for recent procurements.
observations.reason: The Inventory Committee has yet to conduct a complete physical count of Inventories, as they remain fully engaged in completing the physical count of PPE.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the City Accountant to (a) make an analysis of the Due to/from Other Funds accounts to identify the reconciling items needing adjustments and draw the necessary adjusting/correcting entries, and (b) conduct periodic reconciliation of the accounts to avoid misstatements.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting journal entries have been made, however, the reconciliation of accounts is still ongoing.
observations.reason: The accounts involved balances from prior periods which made the reconciliation difficult.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated to the Management that they require the concerned officials of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit Infrastructure Contracts within five (5) days after its perfection duly supported with all the supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, and a transmittal letter containing a list of supporting documents attached to the advance copy of infrastructure contract.
observations.managementAction: The Management has complied with the submission of perfected contracts but not within the prescribed 5-day period and has committed to making their best efforts to improve compliance moving forward.
observations.reason: Competing priorities have hindered the timely execution of the recommended actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO be required to submit the Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) to the Office of the Auditor within twenty-four (24) hours from acceptance of procured goods.
observations.managementAction: The Management has complied with the submission of acceptance and inspection reports but not within the prescribed 24-hour period and has committed to making its best efforts to improve compliance.
observations.reason: Competing priorities have hindered the timely execution of the recommended actions.

observations.recommendation:
1.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to:
complete the conduct of the physical count of all PPE of the Municipality, and
undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

2.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
Concerned contractors, through the Office of the Municipal Engineer, to prepare and submit SWA before the end of the year which shall serve as the basis of the Municipal Engineer in the conduct of inspection and validation of on-going and completed projects, and for the purpose of proper determination of the percentage of completion to be reported in the QRGPPA;
Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to prepare Obligation Request once the contract is awarded and monitor the progress billings based on the subsidiary ledger of the related contract; and
OIC-Municipal Accountant to cause the preparation of necessary journal entry/entries to correct the above-computed understatement of the CIP and Accounts Payable.

4.5	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a. the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the cash allocated for Trust Liability – DRRM from the bank account of General Fund to the bank account of the Trust Fund to facilitate accounting and monitoring of said fund, and avoid possible confusion on the usage of check booklet to be used; and
b. the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary accounting entries after the bank transfer in order to reflect the correct balances of Cash in Bank for each fund and minimize if not to eliminate the balances of the Due to Other Funds and Due from Other Funds accounts.

5.7	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the necessary correcting entries to reclassify the PCSO shares from the Trust Fund to the General Fund for fair presentation of the account in the financial statements and proper utilization; and
b. Ensure appropriate fund classification and treatment of external shares and assistance from National Government Agencies.

6.9	We recommended that management:
a. Through the OIC-Municipal Accountant to initially recognize fund transfer to NGAs for implementation of projects as “Due from NGAs” and not an outright expense.
b. Through the MSWDO to monitor and secure from the Social Welfare Assistant of MMMHMC the quarterly status of fund transfer and utilization report of the fund and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant.
c. Through the OIC-Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to recognize unutilized balance as of year-end as “Due from NGAs” and the corresponding prior period adjustment.
d. Through the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit to the Auditor the fund liquidation reports and its supporting documents.

7.8	We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
instruct the BAC to immediately start the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and
b. require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve. Thus, immediate implementation would enable the municipal government to avail of lesser project/program costs considering the escalating prices of all commodities, hence more benefits could be derived from these funds.

8.10	We recommended that Management:
a. implement the PPAs as planned;
b. address the causes of delay in the implementation of the development projects to maximize the utilization of the fund as intended and ultimately achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in the municipality; and
c. ensure that the unimplemented PPAs that do not partake capital investments in nature be reprogrammed under the allowable expenditure items chargeable against 20% DF.

9.8	We recommended Management to ensure that the 20% DF be optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU as expressly prescribed under the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020.

10.5	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council to stop the practice of appropriating and utilizing the LDRRMF for programs, projects and activities that are not within the four thematic areas of DRRM to improve the LGU’s capacity to prepare and respond to calamities and emergencies.

11.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System Technical Working Group to:
prepare the GAD Plan and Budget to identify priority gender issues to be addressed by the LGU; and
ensure that that the gender issues/GAD mandates are in line with the Updated Gender Equality and Women’s Empowerment Plan 2019-2025 issued by the PCW which serves as reference and guide of LGUs in mainstreaming.

12.7	We recommended that Management ensure that no government funds shall be used for private purposes to ascertain that all resources of the government shall be expended or utilized in accordance with law and regulations and safeguarded against wastage through improper disposition otherwise, disbursements not consistent with existing law, rules and regulations shall be subjected to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance.

13.5	We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Instruct the MBO to only provide appropriations for the payment of Hazard Pay considering the limitations provided by Item 3.3 of DBM-DOH JC No. 1, S. 2016;
b. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to see to it that payment of hazard pay complies with the updated rules and guidelines on the grant of said benefit; and
c. Require the MHO to refund the overpayment of hazard pay for CY 2024 amounting to ₱181,598.40 to avoid disallowance in audit.

14.7	We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Establish the necessary procedures for identifying the personnel exposed to hazards and discuss with the MSWDO for its implementation;
b. Instruct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to check the completeness and propriety of the supporting documents before processing the payments of said benefits; and
c. Require the submission of the documents required for the payment of hazard pay by the personnel of the Office of the MSWD to ascertain the accuracy of payment of ₱289,075.00 and to establish its validity to avoid disallowance in audit.

15.8	We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
a. Instruct the MBO to only provide the maximum appropriations for the payment of Subsistence Allowance considering the limitations provided by Item 8.3 of DBM-DOH JC No. 1, S. 2012 and Item 6.2 of DBM-DSWD JC No. 1, S. 2018; and
b. Instruct the HRMO to prepare the payroll for the grant of Subsistence Allowance in the month succeeding the actual services rendered to ascertain the accuracy and validity of said claims.

16.7	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to ensure that Sales or Commercial Invoices are secured before processing payments to suppliers. This will strengthen compliance with documentary requirements and uphold the integrity of the procurement process.

17.8	We recommended that management grant special cash advances only to duly designated and properly bonded Disbursing Officers or Accountable Officers to prevent placing significant amounts in custody of personnel who are not bonded or instances in which employee of NGA could not be compelled to account for the amounts granted.

18.9	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the LSB to submit the quarterly and annual SEF utilization reports to the DepEd CO, thru appropriate channels and furnish copies to the local sanggunian and the Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG within the prescribed timelines.
19.5	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MHO to fully utilize the PCF in order not to deprive the intended beneficiaries of the benefits that could be derived from these funds.

20.4	We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC through the TWG to conduct the post-qualification process in not more than 12 calendar days from the determination of LCB to avoid delays in the procurement process and in the implementation of the government projects. This is consistent with the timelines previously set under Section 34.8 of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 and now aligned with Section 63 of the RA 
No. 12009, the New Government Procurement Act.

21.6	We recommended that the Municipal Government require the Municipal Engineer to check the contractor’s billing by conducting inspection and verification of actual accomplishments documented through the preparation of a separate Inspection Report before certifying the amount to be paid to the contractor as progress payment. Moreover, the Inspection Report along with the other required documents should be attached to the disbursement vouchers to support the validity and propriety of progress payments.

22.6	We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the BAC to procure goods through competitive public bidding unless conditions set in the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 and now aligned with RA No. 12009, the New Government Procurement Act, on the use of alternative method of procurements have been met, thus ensuring the most advantageous prices available in the market are availed and transparency in the procurement process achieved.

24.6	We recommended that Management:
1) Only grant legally authorized special cash advances as enumerated by Item 3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
2) Pay by check directly to suppliers, dealers, entertainers and the like to ensure that public funds are not exposed to misuse or misappropriation.
3) Use the appropriate account title in recording cash advances to avoid misclassification of accounts in the financial statements.

25.5	We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
intensify the collection of real property taxes; and
issue notice of delinquency to all delinquent taxpayers.

26.6	We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to release the RPT shares of the Barangays within the prescribed period of five days after the end of each quarter regardless of the amount as set forth in Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160 to provide additional funds for the Barangays in the implementation of programs/projects/activities that would benefit their constituents. Further, we recommended that the municipal government to consider the use of an Auto Debit Arrangement (ADA) with the LBP for the release of the said shares to the bank accounts of the barangays which accordingly, reduces clerical work and the time spent by barangay treasurers in processing such shares.

We recommended that Management:
Make a representation with the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) to cause the donation of these PPE items to the end-users such as the Barangays, the Department of Education, and other National Government Agencies. Thereafter, direct the City Accountant to derecognize the donated PPE items from the books to ensure the fair presentation of the PPE account in the financial statements.
Alternatively, if the donation is not feasible, consider formalizing an arrangement through a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or Usufruct Agreement with the Barangays, Department of Education, and National Government Agencies. However, it is imperative that the agreement establishes that the LGU retains control and ownership of the PPE items while allowing the end-users continued use. This arrangement ensures proper accounting treatment while also addressing budgetary constraints faced by end-users in managing repairs, maintenance, and operational costs.

We recommended that Management require:
The City Accounting Office to prepare the proposed adjusting entries to correct all affected accounts, as follows:
To transfer completed projects from CIP to the appropriate PPE accounts:
To recognize depreciation expenses for FY 2024:
The Accounting and Engineering Offices to establish a reporting mechanism to regularly update the status of ongoing Construction-in-Progress (CIP) and completed projects to ensure the timely reclassification of completed projects to their appropriate PPE accounts and facilitate the recognition of these projects even in cases where actual billings from contractors are not yet available.

We reiterated our recommendations that Management:
Direct the Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed;  to reconcile said report with the records of the City Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively;
Direct the CGSO and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly SSMIs to the City Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Instruct the City Accountant to adopt moving average method as costing method for valuation of inventories as prescribed under Section 115, Chapter 7, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that Management:
Require the City Accountant to verify and validate the existence of receivables, determine the debtors, and assess their collectibility. If deemed uncollectible, request authority to write-off the dormant receivable accounts following COA Circular No. 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023. Further, if the receivables lack supporting documents, require the execution of a sworn certification stating the reasons for the loss of documents, and the person/s responsible thereof; and
Ensure that subsidiary ledgers are maintained for receivables in accordance with Section 4(e) of the NGAS to provide detailed information on accounts and facilitate collection efforts in the future.

We recommended that Management:
Require the City Accounting Office to conduct an analysis of the Advances to Contractors account, identifying which advances are still valid, collectible, or require adjustment; and
Evaluate and assess the collectability of long-outstanding advances and, if necessary and applicable, initiate a request for write-off in accordance with COA 2023-008 dated August 17, 2023, which prescribes the guidelines on proper disposition of dormant accounts, particularly for advances that can no longer be substantiated or collected.

We recommended that Management:
Maintain ongoing compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994 in the proper grant, utilization and liquidation of the fund transfers from NGAs;
Ensure strict adherence to the guidelines specified in the respective Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) of each project while considering the principles outlined in P.D. 1445 regarding the management and utilization of government resources; and
Monitor project implementation closely to ensure adherence to the established timeline and that projects remain on track.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the Source Agency to determine the correct disposition of the fund transfer. If no further use is required, the unutilized balance should be reverted to the Source Agency in compliance with existing laws and regulations.

We recommended that Management coordinate with the Source Agency to determine the correct disposition of the fund transfer and secure a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or any formal documentation to establish the fund's purpose, intended use, and conditions for its allocation.

We recommended that Management ensure the transfer of the fund is fully supported by complete and proper documentation in accordance with the requirements outlined in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

We recommended that Management consider making additional efforts to locate the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) or any formal documentation related to the fund transfer, while coordinating closely with the Source Agency, to ensure adherence to the guidelines and proper allocation of the funds. Otherwise, the Management can refer to the provisions in the Section 6(c) of RA 1169 on the allocation of the City’s Lotto and STL share from PCSO in order to provide the necessary assistance and services to the City’s constituents.

We recommended that Management:
Advise the City Accounting Office to reclassify the following amounts from Guaranty/Security Deposits Payable Account to the following accounts:
₱9,733,750.60 to Trust Liabilities
₱7,214,170.50 to Customers’ Deposits Payable
₱45,986,369.13 to Deferred Real Property Tax
Direct the City Accounting Office to conduct a review of the dormant accounts to validate the existence of claims and facilitate their proper disposition or settlement.

We recommended that Management:
Examine the payables which remained outstanding for more than two years to confirm and establish valid claims and submit to the Audit Team pertinent documents to support their validity; otherwise, effect reversion of those accounts that have no valid claims;
Conduct a thorough review of all liability accounts to ensure they meet the criteria for recognition as per the provisions of IPSAS 1, namely: a) existence of a present obligation from past events, b) probability of outflow of resources embodying economic benefits, and c) reliable measurement of the obligation's cost/value; and
Ensure that all obligations are supported by valid claims, bills, relevant records, and valid proofs to warrant payment. This includes maintaining subsidiary ledgers for each creditor with adequate information to justify the existence of such obligations.
We recommended that Management ensure the timely submission of contracts to COA pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure the timely submission of contracts to COA pursuant to Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009; and
Ensure that the APP to be implemented is approved by the HOPE or its duly designated representative.

We recommended that:
The City Treasurer’s Office consistently and completely prepare the required monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms supported with the individual RAAF of each accountable officer and submit the same to the office of the auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month; and
The Accountable Officers, such as collectors and disbursing officers, prepare the individual RAAF and submit it to the City Treasurer for consolidation.

We recommended that the Accountable Officers properly record the transactions in the cashbook on a daily basis to maintain updated balances and reconcile it with the actual cash on hand, rule and foot the transactions at the end of the month, and accomplish the required certification in accordance with the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office and City Treasury Office take immediate action to settle the outstanding premium contributions from prior years by either remitting payment or coordinating with PhilHealth to verify records, determine the cause/s of the discrepancy, and request any necessary adjustments or corrections. Additionally, if adjustments are required, ensure that all correcting entries are accurately recorded in the books to reflect the reconciled balances.

We recommended that the City Accounting Office conduct an analysis of the Due to GSIS account and coordinate with the GSIS to verify records and identify discrepancies. If necessary, ensure that all correcting entries are accurately recorded in the books to reflect the reconciled balances.

We recommended that the Management consider taking the following actions:
Review the unutilized balance to confirm whether it qualifies as savings;
Coordinate with the Department of Agriculture (DA), as the implementing agency of PRDP, to determine the proper disposition or reallocation of the unutilized funds out of the supplemental LGU Equity funds; and
Consider the possibility of reallocating the savings/unutilized funds to other priority projects, in compliance with existing PRDP guidelines.

We recommended that the Management consider the following actions:
Conduct a review of existing job order contracts to determine if the tasks assigned are part of the LGU’s regular functions. If tasks are found to be essential and recurring, consider reassessing the vacant Plantilla positions and overall manpower needs. When necessary, consider hiring existing JO personnel under appropriate employment status (e.g., casual or permanent) to ensure proper classification of accounts and compliance with COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2, Series of 2020 dated October 20, 2020; and
Review the charges to Personnel Services under the Salaries and Wages – Casual/Contractual to verify the proper classification of expenses and draw the correcting journal entries, if necessary, in compliance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 01, 2015.

We recommended that the Management, henceforth, ensure that all Disbursement Vouchers for salary payments to casual employees are duly supported by the required documents under COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, including a copy of the casual appointment bearing the “Received” mark from the Civil Service Commission and a Certification from the Local Chief Executive that the employment is still within the PS limitation.

We recommended that the members of the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) fully implement monitoring tools such as the HGDG and PIMME during GAD planning and budgeting, as well as in the preparation of the accomplishment report. This will ensure the actual expenditures are properly attributed to the GAD budget and that funded programs and projects are genuinely gender-responsive.

We recommended that Management:
Develop and enforce an internal memorandum or directive requiring the concerned office or personnel to submit purchase orders and perfected contracts on time; and
Implement a tracking system for the submission of purchase orders and perfected contracts.

a. The OIC Municipal Accountant reconcile, trace, and identify the
unreconciled balance of CYs 2021 and prior years, prepare a detailed
schedule as to date and particulars of the unrecorded transactions,
and prepare the necessary adjusting entries in the Cash in Bank
Account.

b. The offices of the OIC Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
properly coordinate for the a) review of the contents of outstanding
checks, b) preparation of the list of checks staled and canceled or
checks staled but reissued together with the original disbursement
vouchers and other supporting documents; and c) the list of
unreleased checks as of cutoff date to facilitate accurate preparation
of journal entry vouchers and ensure proper recording of the same.

c. The Mayor’s Office staff timely furnish the OIC Municipal Accountant
of the communications relevant to Fund Transfers.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare adjusting journal entries to
rectify the misclassifications of financial liabilities and ensure proper
review of the recorded transactions per account in accordance with
Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-09 or the Revised Chart of
Accounts for LGUs dated December 1, 2015; and

b. The OIC-Municipal Accountant make a coordinated effort amongst the
offices in the Municipality and review the charges to accounts
payable, after which, derecognize those that are not valid claims to
ensure that only those valid financial liabilities are recognized as
Account Payable.

a. The HRMO in coordination with the Office of the Mayor revisit the
composition of the Inventory Committee and remove the OIC-
Municipal Accountant as the head of the Committee, instead,
implement strategies to ensure that the Office of the GSO is duly
complemented with the needed personnel to fully function and
properly conduct the annual inventory of PPE of the Municipality
instead of the Municipal Accounting Office to comply with the
principles of segregation of duties and internal control;

b. The newly-organized Inventory Committee fast-track the
reconciliation of the PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular
No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, and ensure the immediate and
regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the
Municipality’s PPEs; and

c. The OIC-Municipal Accountant include in the disclosure, the prior
year's unexpended balances in the Notes to Financial Statements and
the details on the receipt and utilization of LDRRMF of the
Municipality using the required format as required under Item 5.1.16
of COA Circular 2012 -002.

We recommended and Management agreed that:

a. The GSO and Municipal Accounting Office follow the inventory
process stated in Section 122, Volume I of the Manual of New
Government Accounting System;

b. The requisitioning units/ concerned offices issued with inventories
maintain a stock card per stock number and prepare an inventory
report every semester to serve as a reference for the GSO during their
conduct of the actual physical count of inventories at each reporting
date;

c. The Municipal Accounting Office and the GSO maintain the Supply
Ledger Cards to reconcile with the Stock Cards, respectively, to
provide a system of check and balance in the recording of inventories
in accordance with the perpetual inventory system, in keeping with
the accounting policies prescribed in the pertinent provisions of the
NGAS Manual, Volume I; and

d. The GSO submit the Report of Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary records of all fund transfers from National Government Agencies. Likewise, direct all concerned personnel who are directly in-charge of the implementation of projects to regularly submit the required reports and complete supporting documents with MOA to the City Accountant and the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that the City Accountant make the proper adjustment in the books for the accumulated share from the PCSO and ensure that the subsequent shares from the PCSO of the City is appropriately accounted as income and recorded using “Share from PCSO (4-04-01-020)” account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the City Treasurer submit a written explanation/justification as to how the discrepancy/shortage occurred, otherwise restitute the discrepancy; monitor the collections and remittances through conduct of daily reconciliation between cashbook balances and actual cash on hand.

We recommended that all designated Collectors shall remit their collections at the end of each business day or the next banking day to avoid misappropriation of cash. For field collectors, in case where travel time from the places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day, turnover of collections shall be made once a week or as soon as the collections reach P5,000.00, whichever comes first; and the City Treasurer or Liquidating Officer shall issue demand letters to Collectors if collections are not remitted at the period required.

We recommended that the Accountable Officers properly record the transactions in their respective cashbooks on a daily basis to maintain updated balances and reconcile it with the actual cash on hand, in accordance with the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended the Management to meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before their inclusion in the EDF and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and promptly implement programs/projects/activities embodied in the 20 percent EDF to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits.

We recommended that the City DRRM Office, in close coordination with the LDRRM Council, include in the subsequent LDRRMFIP the programs, projects and activities that can be implemented during the year through judicious planning and efficient conduct of procurement activities; the City LDRRM Council to convene at least once every three months as required by Section 11 (b) (4) of Republic Act No. 10121 to (a) evaluate/monitor the implementation of LDRRM program, projects and activities; and (b) identify, analyze, and formulate a solution to the factors causing the untimely or slow implementation of the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the Management require the CGSO to prepare and maintain the required (a) Stock Cards and Inventory List of Items for DRRM to properly document, account and control the Inventory, monitoring and issuance of procured welfare goods; and (b) monthly RSMI and furnish a copy thereof to the Accounting Office for recording in the books.

We reiterated that the Management require the City Treasurer’s Office to submit the Reports of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposits daily to the Accounting Office and to facilitate submission to the office of the Auditor within the prescribed period; the City Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office; the City Accountant to submit the financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We recommended and Management agreed that in the course of the conduct of inventory of PPE in 2024, the inventory committee will completely identify insurable properties to be covered by insurance in order for the City to fully comply with Section 5 of the “Property Insurance Law”.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Accounting Office a schedule of unreleased checks at the end of every quarter, and
OIC-Municipal Accountant to restore the cash equivalent of all unreleased checks by debiting Cash in Bank-LCCA and crediting the appropriate payable/liability accounts supported with a working paper at the end of the quarter and by preparing a JEV at the end of the year based on the submitted schedule from the Municipal Treasurer of which said schedule will also be the basis in preparing the JEV in the ensuing year, to reverse the entries made at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to:
complete the conduct of the physical count of all PPE of the Municipality, and
undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
release the RPT shares of the Barangays within the prescribed period of five days after the end of each quarter as set forth in Section 271 (d) of RA No. 7160 to provide additional funds for the Barangays in the implementation of programs/projects/activities that would benefit their constituents; and
facilitate an Auto Debit Arrangement (ADA) with the LBP to release the said shares to the bank accounts of the barangays which accordingly, reduces clerical work and the time spent by barangay treasurers in processing such shares.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 48 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs by adopting the imprest system in accounting for PCF; and
Municipal Budget Officer to prepare the CAFOA and obligate the initial cash advance for PCF as “Other Expenses” and obligate subsequent replenishments with the actual expenses incurred through the paid PCVs to appropriately monitor the balances of existing appropriations for MOOE and to ensure non-incurrence of overdraft.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Engineer to instruct all contractors to see to it that all laborers are provided with complete and appropriate PPEs and cause for the installation of warning devices at the project site whenever necessary in order not to compromise the health and safety of the workers and the public, and henceforth indicate the actual percentage of completion of the pay items in the SWA;
contractors to refund immediately the amount of ₱645,132.25 pertaining to the pay item for CSHP; and
Municipal Accountant to scrutinize the supporting documents of disbursement vouchers and cause for payment only on what is due to the contractors in order not to unduly benefit the latter at the expense of the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to obligate the replenishments of PCF using the actual expense codes and fill out all the data indicated in the CAFOA to ascertain that all obligations are within the budget and are charged to the proper office, function/program/project and account code to avoid the incurrence of overdraft in appropriation and facilitate verification of the Statement of Comparison of Budget versus Actual Amounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Health Officer to fully utilize the PCF in order not to deprive the intended beneficiaries of the benefits that could be derived from these fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor take into consideration the hiring of a Municipal Accountant for a more efficient delivery of services to the constituents.

We reiterated that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to submit to the Auditor at the beginning of the year, a list of all on-going PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year.

We recommended that Management, henceforth, ensure that all revenue and disbursement documents be submitted to the COA not later than the 10th day of the ensuing month to enable timely post audit of these transactions and immediately communicate deficiencies, if any, to management for appropriate corrective action.

We recommended that the Municipal Government, through the Municipal Engineering Office:
strictly require the contractor to prepare and submit the SWA and the Municipal Engineer to check the contractor’s billing by conducting inspection and verification of actual accomplishments documented through the preparation of a separate Inspection Report before certifying the amount to be paid to the contractor as progress payment.; and
submit the Inspection Report along with the other required documents be attached to the disbursement vouchers to support the validity and propriety of progress payments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC through the TWG to conduct the post-qualification process in not more than 12 calendar days from the determination of LCB to avoid delays in the procurement process and the implementation of the government projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to conduct pre-bid conference as specified in the ITB and within the prescribed period as required under Section 22.2 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to give prospective bidders the opportunity to attend said conference and to enable them to clarify matters regarding the bid.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the BAC Chairperson to require the BAC Secretariat to ensure that all the bidding documents are available from the time of advertisement up to the deadline of submission of bids as required in Section 17.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to give prospective bidders ample time to acquire and prepare their bid proposals.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to:
submit justification on the aforementioned lapses;
furnish the Treasury Office a copy of the Invitation to Bid of all the projects subject for bidding as basis for the acceptance of payment of bid documents, and
refrain the issuance of bid documents after the last day of issuance to comply with the set deadline as required under Section 17.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to give equal opportunity to all prospective bidders to participate in the bidding process.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the GAD Focal Point System submit GPB and the corresponding AR to the Audit Team on time as required under Item V of COA Circular No. 2014 – 001 dated March 14, 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the GFPS to fully implement the GAD PPAs to address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the county.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
instruct the BAC to immediately start the procurement process of all the programs/projects funded out of the RA No. 7171 funds which were not yet subjected to public bidding, and
require all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve. Thus, immediate implementation would enable the municipal government to avail of lesser project/program costs considering the escalating prices of all commodities, hence more benefits could be derived from these funds.

We recommended that Management:
implement the PPAs as planned;
address the causes of delay in the implementation of the development projects to maximize the utilization of the fund as intended and ultimately achieve desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes in the municipality; and
ensure that the unimplemented PPAs that do not partake capital investments in nature and for administrative purposes be reprogrammed under the allowable expenditure items chargeable against 20% DF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to:
intensify the collection of real property taxes; and
issue notice of delinquency to all delinquent taxpayers.

We recommended that the Management ensure that the 20% Development Fund be optimally utilized to help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the LGU as expressly prescribed under the DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 2020-01 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to record the following proposed adjusting journal entries if warranted:
To record the adjustment for the Properties being utilized by other agencies
We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) to cause the donation of these PPE items to the Barangays, Department of Education and other National Government Agencies, and thereafter, instruct the City Accountant to derecognize from the books these donated PPE items in order to fairly present the PPE account in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant:
To record the proposed adjusting journal entry if warranted:
To ensure that all subsequent unused balance of funds received from national government agencies shall be returned immediately after completion of program and/or projects pursuant to COA Circular No. 94-013; and
To coordinate with DILG and other source agencies for the proper action to be done with dormant balance of fund transfers totaling P10.002 million.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant:
To record the proposed adjusting journal entry
To ensure that the subsequent shares from the PCSO of the City is appropriately accounted as income and recorded using “Share from PCSO (4-04-01-020)” account pursuant to COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant:
To record the proposed adjusting journal entry:
To monitor the collections and remittances through conduct of daily reconciliation between cashbook balances and actual cash on hand. Any discrepancies identified should be adjusted/restituted so as to avoid incurrence of negative balances/overage or shortage due to over or under-remittances and come up with updated, more accurate and reliable balance of accountabilities in the Cashbooks.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Treasurer’s Office to prepare the required monthly Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms supported by the individual RAAF of each accountable officers and submit the same to the office of the Auditor not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct that:
All designated Collectors shall remit their collections to the City Treasurers at the end of each business day or the next banking day to avoid misappropriation of cash. For field collectors, in case where travel time from the places of assignment to the Treasurer’s Office is more than one day, turnover of collections shall be made once a week or as soon as the collections reach P5,000.00, whichever comes first; and
The City Treasurer or Liquidating Officer shall issue demand letters to Collectors if collections are not remitted at the period required.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the Accountable Officers to properly record the transactions in their respective cashbooks on a daily basis to maintain updated balances and reconcile it with the actual cash on hand, in accordance with the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary records of all fund transfers from National Government Agencies. Likewise, direct all concerned personnel who are directly in-charge of the implementation of projects to regularly submit the required reports and complete supporting documents with MOA to the City Accountant and the Office of the Auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the concerned official to meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before their inclusion in the EDF and Annual Investment Plan (AIP) and promptly implement programs/projects/activities embodied in the 20% EDF to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 1 dated November 4, 2020.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct:
The City DRRM Office, in close coordination with the LDRRM Council, include in the subsequent LDRRMFIP the programs, projects and activities that can be implemented during the year through judicious planning and efficient conduct of procurement activities; and
The City LDRRM Council to convene at least once every 3 months as required by Section 11 (b) (4) of Republic Act No. 10121 to (a) evaluate/monitor the implementation of LDRRM program, projects and activities; and (b) identify, analyze, and formulate a solution to the factors causing the untimely or slow implementation of the LDRRMFIP.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Require the CGSO to prepare and maintain the required (a) Stock Cards and Inventory List of Items for DRRM to properly document, account and control the inventory, monitoring and issuance of procured welfare goods; and (b) monthly RSMI and furnish a copy thereof to the Accounting Office for recording in the books;
Direct the CGSO and the City Accounting Office to conduct periodic reconciliation of accounting and property records to identify discrepancy/ies, if any, for verification and adjustment in the books; and
Consider the possibility of hiring additional workforce for CGSO, for them to be able to fully carry out their duties and functions as required by existing laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the concerned officials the following:
Comply with the guidelines and procedures for the “One-time Cleansing of PPE Accounts” prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 as follows:
Creation of Inventory Committee, whose members are temporarily relieved from their regular duties, to complete the conduct of inventory;
Recognition of PPE items found at station; and
Disposal/derecognition of non-existing/missing PPE items.
Maintain and regularly update property cards, subsidiary ledgers, property ledger cards, stock cards and other subsidiary records in conformity with Section 13, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Stop providing depreciation charges for unserviceable properties and facilitate their disposal in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City Accountant to prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for the road and its components namely: (1) road lot, (2) road pavement, (3) drainage and slope protection structures and (4) other miscellaneous structure.  Further, prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation of each road component, and disclose the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements;
City GSO to prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network, maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, and maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
City Accountant and the City GSO to secure from the City Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken for them to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to (a) make an analysis of the Due to/Due from Other Funds accounts to identify the reconciling items needing adjustments and draw the necessary adjusting/correcting entries, and (b) conduct periodic reconciliation of the accounts to avoid misstatements.

We reiterated that the City Mayor require the concerned officials of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit the Purchase Orders and Infrastructure Contracts within five (5) days after its perfection duly supported with all the supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009, and a transmittal letter containing a list of supporting documents attached to the advance copy of infrastructure contract.
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the GSO to submit the Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) to the Office of the Auditor within 24 hours from acceptance of procured goods.

We reiterated that the City Mayor:
Require the City Treasurer’s Office to submit the Reports of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposit daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 43 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
Instruct the City Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and
Require the City Accountant to submit the financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We recommended and management agreed that in the course of the conduct of inventory of PPE in 2024, the inventory committee will completely identify insurable properties to be covered by insurance in order for the City to fully comply with Section 5 of the “Property Insurance Law”.

1.6	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the appropriate adjusting entries to reclassify the affected PPE in accordance with COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 with due consideration of the issuance of the Inventory Custodianship Slip to establish accountability of the end-user.

2.9	We reiterated our recommendation that the ICO – Municipal Accountant:
Draw an adjusting journal entry to recognize the understatement representing each RPT Receivable and SET Receivable account the amount of ₱2,713,378.09 and ₱3,689,892.78, respectively for calendar year 2023 and tax due from delinquent accounts of previous years, and credit to Deferred Real Property Tax Income and Deferred Special Education Tax Income; and
Disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the real property tax delinquent accounts as of December 31, 2023.

4.9	We reiterated our recommendation to the Management to:
a.	Direct the Accountant to prioritize the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit them to this office for verification.
b.	Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to quarterly reconcile the Cash in Bank account balances with the Treasurer’s cashbooks.

5.7	We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office record the transfer of the cost of completed projects booked as PPE under the Trust Fund to the General Fund in accordance with Section 104, 1(i) of the MNGAS or LGUs, Vol. 1 and corresponding depreciation expenses shall then be provided in the books following IPSAS 17; and

6.8	We recommended that the ICO-Municipal Accountant to:
a.	Prepare an adjusting journal entry to close the income and expense to Project Equity or the Due to NGA account to fairly present the net income account in the Statement of Financial Performance.
b.	Record transactions in the Trust Fund books as prescribed in the IPSAS 1 and New Government Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units.

7.7 	We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office and the General Services Office in coordination with the new members of the Inventory Committee complete the conduct of inventory, prepare RPCPPE properly classified per PPE item, fast-track the reconciliation of the PPE accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020, and ensure the immediate and regular reconciliation of records of both offices pertaining to the Municipality’s PPEs

8.6	We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office prepare and maintain a detailed ledger card for each class of PPE in soft copies, coordinate with the GSO, and review the composition and balances of each PPE item to reconcile the noted variance of P 566,851,904.38 between the balances per supporting schedule and as presented in the Statement of Financial Position to comply with Section 114(2) of PD 1445.

9.7	We recommended that the OIC- Municipal Accountant provide additional disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2023 the required details/information for the Property, Plant and Equipment including Total Road Network System in accordance with IPSAS provisions and COA Circular No. 2016-004 dated September 30, 2016.

10.10	We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant, GSO Designate, and the OIC-Municipal Assessor reconcile their records to make the necessary adjusting entries to record all land owned by the Municipality, in consonance with Section 111 of Presidential Decree 1445 and International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) No. 1 and 17.

11.6	We recommended that the Municipal Development Planning Council revisit the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 dated November 4, 2020, in preparing the 20 percent Annual Investment Plan to include only PPAs which are instrumental to the attainment of development projects of the Municipality which will benefit the general populace.

12.9	We recommended that the Management strictly comply with the provisions in Article 454(d) of the Rules and Regulation Implementing RA No. 7160, refrain from appropriating funds in lump-sum or generic terms, and ensure that projects are described with sufficient details in order not to cause more delays in implementing the project, in accordance with Section 22 (c) of RA 7160 and pertinent rulings of GR No. 175527, December 8, 2008.

13.9	We recommended that:
The Municipal Development Planning Council improve the budget preparation process to come up with a more comprehensive AIP and budget identifying projects as embodied in the Municipality’s duly approved local development plans and medium-term and annual investment programs which are being harmonized with the Regional Development Plan and the Philippine Development Plans; and
The Municipal Development Planning Council observe transparency and accountability in the publication of reports in the FDP Portal by correctly reporting the actual PPAs implemented by the Municipality.

14.5	We recommended that Management strictly observe the timely submission of disbursement vouchers for the grant of cash advance for CF with complete supporting documents as prescribed in Section 6.1.8.4 of COA-DBM-DILG-GCG-DND Joint Circular 2015-01.

15.11	We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds Accounts as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015, to correct the balances of the said accounts in the statement of financial position of the Municipality.

17.8	We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office:
1.  Maintain and regularly update the General Ledgers and Subsidiary Ledgers following the prescribed format in accordance with NGAS Manual for Local Government Units; and
2. 	Maintain a hardbound copy of ledgers with updated electronic copies to ensure accessibility and security of records.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:

City Accountant to prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for the road and its components namely: (1) road lot, (2) road pavement, (3) drainage and slope protection structures and (4) other miscellaneous structure.  Further, prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation of each road component, and disclose the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements;

observations.recommendation:

Direct the City Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventoriesas prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and

Strictly enjoin compliance with the above-cited rules for the fair presentation of inventory and related expense accounts in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management should meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Program and promptly implement programs/projects/activities embodied in the 20 percent LDF in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG & DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated 22 February 2017.
observations.managementAction: Balances from implemented projects will be reprogrammed while unfinished projects from EDF of prior periods will be continued with urgency to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management make representations with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to facilitate the enactment of the Market Code and renewal of lease contracts of market stallholders to maximize the revenue earning capacity of the City.
observations.managementAction: Management informed us that a new Market Code will be enacted which will cover a comprehensive and updated policy for market operation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor issue a directive for the City Treasurer’s Office to intensify efforts in the collection of taxes due and collectible by sending Notice of Delinquency to RPT Taxpayers and form a committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.  We also recommended imposing the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code in the collections of delinquent taxes.

We recommended that the Management:
Assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.
For CY 2022, prepare a Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) and submit the same to the COA Audit Team at least ten calendar days before the scheduled start of inventory taking activities in compliance with the pertinent provisions of Section 5 of the Circular.
Fill up the Inventory Count Form and submit the same to the Property Unit for verification. The form will then be used by the Inventory Committee as basis in the preparation of its PIP and will be considered as part of their inventory working paper.
Moreover, the Inventory Committee record/document the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group and will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count.
Prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) during physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts.
Ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.
Request the presence of the Audit Team to witness the inventory taking activities of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Designated Municipal Accountant to prepare Bank    Reconciliation Statements.
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Treasurer to reconcile the Cash in Bank account balances with the Treasurer’s cash books.
Looking forward, conduct quarterly reconciliation of such balances so that the adjustments made or errors detected either by the Treasury or Accounting Office will be adjusted and posted in their respective books.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Accountant the duly certified list of taxpayers and the tax due at the beginning of each year, so the same will be the basis in setting up the RPT Receivable.
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to segregate the delinquent taxes prior to CY 2002 for the Municipal Accountant to record them directly to Real Property Tax Income/Special Education Tax Income account when there are payments made.

We recommended that the Management prioritize the provision of depreciation to the properties under the General Fund and all properties purchased prior years to fairly present the account balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the Designated Municipal Accountant to draw an adjusting entry to correct the erroneous entry made for the payment of Road Right of Way as follows:
Construction in Progress –
Infrastructure Assets 			₱1,685,388.00
Land Improvements				₱1,430,388.00
Construction in Progress –
Buildings & Other Structures 			      255,000.00
Direct also the Designated Municipal Accountant to close the Construction in Progress account for the completed projects and transfer it to the appropriate asset account and recognize the related depreciation expense.

We recommended that the Management:
Revisit the provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 to reinvigorate the agency on its duties and responsibilities on the accounting and reporting guidelines of local roads;
Prioritize the conduct of physical count of properties by directing the Accounting, Engineering, and GSO offices to coordinate with each other as regards the recording and reporting of local roads to ensure that local roads are properly tagged with their respective road network IDs and other technical details are properly noted; and finally
Direct the Municipal Accountant to include the details of local roads in the notes to financial statements

We recommended that the Management:
Advise the ICO- Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation and submission of financial reports by availing the eNGAS and eBudget from the COA to facilitate systematic generation of financial reports;
Moreover, register from LBP we-access application to facilitate the prompt preparation of Bank Reconciliation Statements, if warranted; and
Strictly adhere to the prescribed period for submission of the required reports to facilitate immediate action on deficiencies noted.

We recommended that the Management:
Submit justification/s or reason/s for the non-submission of SCABAA and SAAOB for CY 2021.
Require the Designated Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer for the submission of SCABAA and SAAOB for CY 2021.
Looking forward, ensure submission of the aforementioned reports by the concerned department heads and/or cause the suspension of the payment of their salaries until they shall have complied with the requirements in pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that the Management:
Ascertain and address the causes of the delay in the submission of transaction documents to the Office of the Auditor within the reglementary period.
Henceforth, ensure that the required reports, disbursement vouchers, official receipts and other pertinent documents are completely submitted to the Accounting Office not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month for the immediate recording in the books of accounts.
Further, give priority to the timely preparation and submission of the transaction documents for audit in such form and within the period prescribed by the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that the Management:
Require the HRMO to strictly monitor the personnel assigned in the maintenance and monthly updating of employee’s leave cards and review the entries/computation in the leave cards to ensure accurate recording;
Submit all the lacking documents for review and reference in audit; and
Looking forward, strictly comply with the provisions under Section 4 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-01 dated June 4, 2012 by attaching a documentary checklist on the face of the DV, and ensure complete attachments before payment.

We recommended that the Management immediately submit the original copies of the lacking supporting documents and refund excess travel claims of P7,200.00 as indicated in the attached Annex P and strictly comply with the provisions under Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension.

We recommended that the Management:
Direct the MDRRMO to follow the prescribed format of the DRRMP as recommended by the OCD as well as the LDRRMFIP and Report on Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund presented as Annexes A and B of COA Circular 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012; and
Conduct thorough planning in the preparation of DRRM projects to determine properly what to include in the PPAs to make it more transparent to enhance accountability that may improve the LGU’s absorptive capacity and the enhancement of their disaster preparedness and response capabilities.
Require the supplier to stop issuing official receipt if payment is not yet made. Instead, issue charge invoice and statement of account to inform the Municipality of its obligation.
observations.managementAction:
Fully implemented. 

Complied with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the posting requirements provided under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 on the projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.
observations.managementAction: FI.
