For future transactions, attach a documentary checklist per disbursement voucher to ensure that every transaction has complete supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 1.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

observations.managementAction: If thru admin implementation, there will be no indirect costs involved (12-15% of direct cost), contractors profit (8%), VAT, OCM.

observations.recommendation:
Immediately submit the approved DV together with the lacking supporting documents and submit the approved contract to COA for review; and

Moving forward, direct the requesting office/unit to attach in the face of the DV the documentary checklist per COA Circular 2012-001 according to their nature of transactions and for the Accountant to check to ensure completeness of supporting documents before payment.

Submit the lacking documents as enumerated in Section 13 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in support of the road right of way expenditures; and

For future transactions, attach a documentary checklist per disbursement voucher to ensure that said transaction has complete supporting documents.
Strictly comply with the provisions under Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 and submit the lacking supporting documents for reference in audit.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of Department of Interior and Local Government and Department of Budget and Management Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017, that charges to 20% Development Fund shall be for capital expenditure projects which contribute to the socio-economic development and environment management to preclude disallowances in an audit and expenses which are administrative in nature should be charged against the General Fund.

observations.reason: The amount of delinquency for CY 2021 has reduced to ₱85,425.79

observations.recommendation: Instruct the BAC   Secretariat to ensure that the Notice of Award, Notice to proceed and Approved Contract for the procurements undertaken by the LGU are posted in the PhilGEPS.
Instruct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that Performance Security is posted prior to the signing of contract to protect the Municipality in case the contractor will fail to perform its obligations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Inventory Committee to:
complete the conduct of the physical count of all PPE of the Municipality, and
undertake all other procedures prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 to come up with the reconciled and correct balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
the Sangguniang Bayan appropriate funds for the fees needed for the transfer of ownership from the seller to the Municipal Government of Santiago, the three parcels of land acquired in 2010, 2018 and 2019 with Tax Declarations that are still under the name of the seller and the titling of the same including the two parcels of land acquired many years ago;
the Municipal Mayor require the OIC – Municipal Assessor to initiate the transfer from the name of the seller to the Municipality of Santiago of the Tax Declaration of the three parcels of land previously mentioned, and
the Municipal Mayor require the OIC – Municipal Assessor to obtain TCT/OCT of all the parcels of land acquired by the municipality.
Thus, absolute ownership over these properties would be established preventing other possible claimants of the land and potential incurrence of additional litigation expenses will be avoided.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the quarterly financial statements on time in order to provide a snapshot of the municipality's financial health, giving Management insight into its performance, operations, and cash flow and timely corrective measures for any deficiencies or irregularities discovered would be discontinued and rectified.
We also recommended that in order for the Accounting Office to prepare the required reports, the Municipal Mayor consider the hiring of a new Municipal Accountant since the incumbent Municipal Accountant is the only one qualified for the position of Municipal Assessor.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO Designate to accomplish all the necessary and relevant information required in the Purchase Order so that the supplier will deliver the goods/items in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contract and impose liquidated damages in case the supplier fails to deliver the items on time.  Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all POs are properly accomplished before processing claims for payment of suppliers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman who is also the Municipal Engineer to furnish the Auditor a copy of all contracts together with the required supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution/perfection in order for the Audit Team to perform timely review of the same so that deficiencies, if any, could be communicated to management for immediate and appropriate action, and possible sanctions to employees concerned for unjustified inability to comply will be accordingly avoided.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Engineer to inform the Auditor in writing on the posting of infrastructure projects billboards within ten days after the award of the contract and comply with the submission of monthly report of on-going and completed contracts as required under COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 20, 2013 in order to promote transparency in the implementation of infrastructure projects and to ensure that the right of the people to information on matters of public concern is addressed.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to deduct 10% retention money on progress billings of infrastructure projects or require the contractor to post the required warranty securities in order that any discovered defects and third party liabilities within the liability period shall be duly covered, thus the interest of the government is protected.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer for heavy equipment and the Designated Driver for service vehicles to prepare the Monthly Report of Official Travels and the Report of Fuel Consumption for all vehicles/heavy equipment owned by the municipality and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for the latter to hand over the same to the Auditor within the first ten days of the succeeding month, thus timely monitoring and verification of official travels of every vehicle/heavy equipment and the reasonableness of the fuel consumed and deficiencies and/or irregularities if any, will be immediately communicated to Management for appropriate action.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Supply Officer I to:
work out for the conversion of the vehicles with conduction stickers and green plates into government plates and apply with the Land Transportation Office (LTO) for the issuance of plate for the two units of trucks, and
cause for the proper markings of all vehicles owned by the municipality as prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61 in order to prevent unauthorized or improper use thereof.

We recommended that Management fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan by facilitating compliance with the DENR requirements for the operation of the landfill; imposing stricter implementation of waste segregation at source, and conducting information dissemination/imposing penalties for burning.
We also recommended that in close coordination with the barangays, Management adopt a more stringent approach for the implementation of the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 in order to safeguard public health from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all concerned Department Heads to facilitate the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.

We recommended that henceforth, Management through the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer intensify and closely monitor the implementation of proposed priority programs/projects/activities under the 20% DF in order to deliver on time the benefits the constituents could derive therefrom and achieve socio-economic development, environmental management outcomes and broader scope of development.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Point Officer to submit the GPB to the COA Audit Team within five days from receipt of the DILG – endorsed GPB, and the GAD AR duly received by the DILG within five days from the end of January of the ensuing year.  Hence, deficiencies if any, could be communicated to Management on time for appropriate corrective action.

We recommended that:
The City Treasurer should send written notice to the payee of the existence of the check, at least one month before a check becomes stale in compliance to Section 59 Volume 1 of MNGAS for LGUs;
For improperly reported stale checks, the Office of the City Treasurer and the Office of the City Accountant, provide evidence that the checks are not outstanding; that they have been voided and no obligation exist; and
The City Accountant should prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended the following:
Comply with the guidelines and procedures for the “One-time Cleansing of PPE Accounts” prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006 as follows:
Creation of Inventory Committee, whose members are temporarily relieved from their regular duties, to complete the conduct of inventory;
Recognition of PPE items found at station; and
Disposition/derecognition of non-existing/missing PPE items.
Direct the concerned City Officials to maintain and regularly update property cards, subsidiary ledgers, property ledger cards, stock cards and other subsidiary records in conformity with Section 13, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Stop providing depreciation charges for unserviceable properties and facilitate their disposal in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City Accountant to prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for the road and its components namely: (1) road lot, (2) road pavement, (3) drainage and slope protection structures and (4) other miscellaneous structure.  Further, prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation of each road component, and disclose the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements;
City GSO to (1.) Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network, (2.) Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, and (3.) Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
City Accountant and the City GSO to secure from the City Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken for them to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.

We recommended that Management:
Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed;  to reconcile said report with the records of the City Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively;
Direct the City Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventoriesas prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Instruct the City General Services Officer (GSO) and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly SSMIs to the City Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that the City Treasurer should furnish the City Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and the City Accountant should set-up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list.

We recommended that the City Government of Santiago undertake a collective effort to revise the Schedule of Fair Market Values (SFMV) of real properties to update the real property tax assessments in compliance with Section 219 of RA 7160.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit Infrastructure Contracts and Purchase Orders within five (5) days after its perfection duly supported with all the supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and a transmittal letter containing a list of supporting documents attached to the advance copy of infrastructure contract/purchase order.
We recommended that the GSO be required to submit the Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) to the Office of the Auditor within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance of procured goods.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Department to analyze and ascertain the validity of long outstanding payables.  Those found valid should be paid immediately.  On the other hand, recorded payables without valid documentation and which have been outstanding for two or more years and against which no actual claim had been filed should be, with prior concurrence of the Sangguniang Panlungsod, reverted to Government Equity, to clear payable accounts.  Henceforth, only obligations with proper and complete documentation should be recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended and management agreed that in the course of the conduct of inventory of PPE in 2021, the inventory committee will completely identify insurable properties to be covered by insurance in order for the City to fully comply with Section 5 of the “Property Insurance Law”.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management should meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Program and promptly implement programs/projects/activities embodied in the 20 percent LDF in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG & DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated 22 February 2017.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary records of all fund transfers from National Government Agencies.   Likewise, direct all concerned personnel who are directly in-charge of the implementation of projects to regularly submit the required reports and complete supporting documents to the City Accountant and the Office of the Auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management make representations with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to facilitate the enactment of the Market Code and renewal of lease contracts of market stallholders to maximize the revenue earning capacity of the City.

We recommended that Management direct the Local School Board to appropriate reasonable amounts from the SEF for the implementation of projects, programs and activities as prescribed under Section 272 of RA 7160 for the improvement and development of public schools and its students in the City of Santiago.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the SCGADC and all the departments shall submit regularly GAD Accomplishment Reports with the corresponding actual costs for particular programs/projects for review and evaluation of accomplishments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: 

require the personnel in-charge of distribution and the Municipal Accountant to submit written justifications/explanations on the aforementioned lapses and submit the original copy of the distribution lists duly certified by the barangay captain as presented in the annex attached to the AOM issued to management, otherwise the transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit;

instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that for transactions of the same nature, the original copy of the distribution list of welfare goods or any items distributed to constituents be certified by the head of office making the distribution and the same must be attached to the disbursement voucher to be submitted to COA, and

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor issued Memorandum No. 16 dated July 2, 2021 instructing the Municipal Budget Officer to comply with the recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to stop the practice of splitting contracts just to avoid public bidding. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Accountant coordinate with the City Engineering Office to trace the composition of CIP accounts and draw the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers to transfer the completed projects to appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that withholding taxes are withheld from payments of honoraria and allowances and the same should be remitted within the prescribed period to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 11-2018.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to observe the timely rendition of accounts to facilitate the preparation of financial statements and conduct of timely audit, and submit to the Office of the Auditor the 287 unsubmitted DV’s for audit.
We recommended that City Treasurer stop the irregular practice of pre-signing of checks and to strictly comply with Section 83 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Planning and Development Office, in coordination with the GAD Focal Person, to spearhead the updating of the GAD Database which will aid in the gender analysis and fund utilization of GAD Funds for existing and emerging GAD issues.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary records of all fund transfers from National Government Agencies.   Likewise, direct all concerned personnel who are directly in-charge of the implementation of projects to regularly submit the required reports and complete supporting documents to the City Accountant and the Office of the Auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.
observations.managementAction: They partially complied with the submission of records for audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Government of Santiago undertake a collective effort to revise the Schedule of Fair Market Values (SFMV) of real properties to update the real property tax assessments in compliance with Section 219 of RA 7160.
observations.reason: Due to the economic impact of the COVID 19 pandemic, Management postponed the revision of RPT assessments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the concerned officials of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit Infrastructure Contracts and Purchase Orders within five (5) days after its perfection duly supported with all the supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and a transmittal letter containing a list of supporting documents attached to the advance copy of infrastructure contract/purchase order.
observations.managementAction: The management instructed the concerned LGU officials to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the GSO be required to submit the Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) to the Office of the Auditor within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance of procured goods.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that they have already instructed the GSO Office to submit to COA Office within 24 hours the Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) from acceptance of procured goods.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management should meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Program and promptly implement programs/projects/activities embodied in the 20 percent LDF in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG & DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated 22 February 2017.
observations.managementAction: The total unutilized balance of ₱199.619M of prior years’ 20% EDF for CY 2008 to 2018 will be subjected to review and evaluation. Balances from implemented projects will be reprogrammed while unfinished projects from EDF of prior periods will be continued with urgency to comply with the audit recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that Management make representations with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to facilitate the enactment of the Market Code and renewal of lease contracts of market stallholders to maximize the revenue earning capacity of the City.
observations.managementAction: The management commented that they have already submitted letter to the SP to expedite the immediate adaption and passage of the Santiago City Market Code to address the audit recommendation and eventually maximize the revenue earning capacity of the City.
None

Unimplemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to record in the agency books all procurement of supplies for stock as Inventories and issuances thereof to the appropriate expense accounts duly supported with Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) in order to reflect the correct balances of affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to furnish the Auditor a copy of all contracts together with the required supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution in order for the Audit Team to perform timely review of the same and conduct inspection of the completed projects so that deficiencies could immediately be detected, communicated and corrected and sanctions for unjustified failure to comply will be accordingly avoided.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Records disclosed that management did not furnish the Auditor a copy of contracts together with the supporting documents within five working days after perfection thereof.  These were only submitted to the Auditor as attachments to the disbursement vouchers for post audit.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to inform the Auditor in writing on the posting of infrastructure projects billboards within ten (10) days after the award of the contract and comply with the submission of monthly report of on-going and completed contracts as required under COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 20, 2013.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: Records showed that management did not inform the Auditor in writing regarding the posting of infrastructure project billboards and did not submit monthly report of on-going and completed contracts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management henceforth ensure that all revenue and disbursement documents including the monthly bank reconciliation statements shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit not later than the 10th day of the following month.  

Likewise, submit  the quarterly trial balances until the 20th day after the end of each quarter. Moreover, it is strongly recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause for the suspension of the payment of salary of the Municipal Accountant if she fails to submit the required reports and records on time as provided under Section 122 (2) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause for the immediate remittance of the tax deficiencies aggregating P1,522,477.90 in order to avoid further interests and penalties, anyway, the amount will redound to the benefit of the municipality in the form of Internal Revenue Allotments (IRA).

Further, we recommended that the interest and penalties in connection with the late remittance of taxes shall not be charged against funds of the municipality but rather a personal liability of the Municipal Accountant who was in charge in the withholding and remittance of correct amount of tax due.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied

Verification of financial statements disclosed that only a portion of the amount was remitted to BIR.  Reiterated in Observation No. 20, pages 70-71 of the report.

Implemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Chairman agreed to follow strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to attain competitive bidding and to guarantee that the programs and projects in the municipality may not be put at risk during the implementation of the infrastructure projects and the delivery of goods.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied

Post audit of disbursements of infrastructure projects disclosed  some infractions in the IRR of RA 9184.

Reiterated in Observation No. 18, pages 67-68 of the report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan by facilitating compliance with the DENR requirements for the operation of the landfill; speeding up the construction of access road; imposing stricter implementation of waste segregation at source; conducting information dissemination/imposing fines on burning; and ensuring the procurement of garbage compactor. 

We also recommended that in close coordination with the barangays, Management adopt a more stringent approach for the implementation of the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 in order to safeguard public health from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied

Management also informed the Audit Team that they are in the process of complying with the documents  required by DENR for the operation of the sanitary landfill.

Unimplemented
Interview disclosed that garbage burning is resorted to by  the barangay residents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that henceforth, Management consider the Return on Investment (ROI) or the costs of the assets plus reasonable amount of profit for renting out such facilities in determining the minimum rental fees for Investment Properties so as to generate income which is the very purpose of these investments and not to incur loss to the detriment of the municipality.
observations.managementAction: None
observations.reason: The municipality did not venture  into such kind of investment during the year under review.  However, management did not consider the ROI in determining the rental fee of stalls in the public market as discussed in Observation No. 26, pages 80-83 of the report.

Further, the Municipal Treasurer should collect the unpaid stall rentals.

It is also recommended that management give priority attention on the cleanliness of the public market in order to attract more customers.

Unimplemented

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed not to process claims for payment unless these are completely documented. Likewise, the Municipal Engineer agreed to submit the lacking documents of infrastructure projects as required in the Notices of Suspensions. Further, he agreed to see to it that all infrastructure projects are completed in accordance with plans and specifications and the computation of the programs of work which is the basis of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) should be reasonable in order to avoid disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied
observations.reason:
Post audit of disbursements disclosed lacking documents.  It is noteworthy to mention however that there was a significant improvement in the documentation of disbursements as compared to the previous years.  

Reiterated in Observation No. 27, pages 84-85 of the report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Treasurer should furnish the City Accountant with a certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year; and the City Accountant should set-up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based on the certified list.

We recommended that Management:
Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed;  to reconcile said report with the records of the City Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RPCI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively;
Direct the City Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories as prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Instruct the City General Services Officer (GSO) and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly SSMIs to the City Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We recommended that Management make representation with the Sangguniang Panlungsod (SP) to cause the donation of these PPE items to Barangays, Department of Education and other national government agencies, and thereafter, instruct the City Accountant to derecognize from the books these donated PPE items in order to fairly present the PPE account in the Financial Statements.
We reiterated the following recommendations:
Direct the Santiago City Inventory and Disposal Committee to (i) conduct a complete physical count annually as required; (ii) intensify efforts to reconcile the result of physical count with property and accounting records and (iii) recommend for the adjustment of any noted discrepancy in compliance with Paragraph 2 of Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs;
Direct the concerned City Officials to maintain and regularly update property cards, subsidiary ledgers, property ledger cards, stock cards and other subsidiary records in conformity with Section 13, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Stop providing depreciation charges for unserviceable properties and facilitate their disposal in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
City Accountant to prepare and maintain Local Road Network Ledger Cards for the road and its components namely: 1. road lot, 2. road pavement, c. drainage and slope protection structures and 4. other miscellaneous structure.  Further, prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of depreciation of each road component, and disclose the Road Network System in the Notes to Financial Statements.
City GSO to 1. Prepare and submit the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network,  2. Maintain a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components, and 3. Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map.
City Accountant and the City GSO to secure from the City Engineer the complete description and cost segregation of road components of every road project undertaken for them to have a basis in recording the road networks in the Local Road Network Ledger Card and Local Road Network Property Card, respectively.

We recommended that the City Government of Santiago undertake a collective effort to revise the Schedule of Fair Market Values (SFMV) of real properties to update the real property tax assessments in compliance with Section 219 of RA 7160.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit Infrastructure Contracts and Purchase Orders within five (5) days after its perfection duly supported with all the supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and a transmittal letter containing a list of supporting documents attached to the advance copy of infrastructure contract/purchase order.
We recommended that the GSO be required to submit the Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) to the Office of the Auditor within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance of procured goods.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Department to:
Reconcile their records of Accounts Payable account to arrive at a correct/reconciled balances and draw the necessary adjusting entries on the errors committed promptly; and
Analyze and ascertain the validity of long outstanding payables.  Those found valid should be paid immediately.  On the other hand, recorded payables without valid documentation and which have been outstanding for two or more years and against which no actual claim had been filed should be, with prior concurrence of the Sangguniang Panglungsod, reverted to Government Equity, to clear payable accounts.  Henceforth, only obligations with proper and complete documentation should be recorded in the books of accounts.

We recommended that the City Accountant coordinate with the City Engineering Office to trace the composition of CIP accounts and draw the necessary Journal Entry Vouchers to transfer the completed projects to appropriate asset accounts.

We recommended that the City Accountant ensure that withholding taxes are withheld from payments of honoraria and allowances and the same should be remitted within the prescribed period to comply with BIR Revenue Regulation No. 11-2018.

We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to observe the timely rendition of accounts to facilitate the preparation of financial statements and conduct of timely audit, and submit to the Office of the Auditor the 287 unsubmitted DV’s for audit.
We recommended that City Treasurer stop the irregular practice of pre-signing of checks and to strictly comply with Section 83 of the Local Treasury Operations Manual.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the City Planning and Development Office, in coordination with the GAD Focal Person, to spearhead the updating of the GAD Database which will aid in the gender analysis and fund utilization of GAD Funds for existing and emerging GAD issues.

We recommended and management agreed that in the course of the conduct of inventory of PPE in 2020, the inventory committee will completely identify insurable properties to be covered by insurance in order for the City to fully comply with Section 5 of the “Property Insurance Law”.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management should meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Program and promptly implement programs/projects/activities embodied in the 20 percent LDF in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG & DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated 22 February 2017.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary records of all fund transfers from National Government Agencies.   Likewise, direct all concerned personnel who are directly in-charge of the implementation of projects to regularly submit the required reports and complete supporting documents to the City Accountant and the Office of the Auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management make representations with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to facilitate the enactment of the Market Code and renewal of lease contracts of market stallholders to maximize the revenue earning capacity of the City.

We recommended that the City Mayor issue a directive for the City Treasurer’s Office to intensify efforts in the collection of taxes due and collectible by sending Notice of Delinquency to RPT Taxpayers and form a committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.  We also recommended imposing the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code in the collections of delinquent taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant henceforth be guided by the Standard Chart of Accounts in the budget preparation and in the recording of financial transactions in the books in order to reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entry by debiting Due to BIR and crediting Advances to Officers and Employees by P229,093.23 in order to present fairly the balances of these accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that:
a).  the Sangguniang Bayan appropriate funds for the fees needed for the transfer of ownership from the seller to the Municipal Government of Santiago, the three parcels of land acquired in 2010, 2018 and 2019 with Tax Declarations that are still under the name of the seller  and the titling  of the same including the two  parcels of land acquired many years ago;
b).   the Municipal Mayor require the OIC Municipal Assessor to work out for the transfer from the name of the seller to the Municipality of Santiago of the Tax Declaration of the three parcels of land previously mentioned, and
c). 	the Municipal Mayor require the OIC-Municipal Assessor to obtain TCT/OCT of all the parcels of land acquired by the municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections turned over to her daily or not later than the next banking day. Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the Municipal Treasurer to require all the collectors that at the close of each business day, they accomplish all the data in the RCD and turn over all their collections to  her in order that the same will be deposited intact.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the quarterly financial statements on time in order to maximize usefulness of the financial information and would enable Management to make timely corrective measures should errors, deficiencies or irregularities are discovered through the financial statements.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider the hiring of a new Municipal Accountant since she is the only one qualified for the position of Municipal Assessor.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to record in the agency books all procurement of supplies for stock as Inventories and issuances thereof to the appropriate expense accounts duly supported with RIS and SSMI in order to reflect the correct balance of affected accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
require the personnel in-charge of distribution and the Municipal Accountant to submit written justifications/explanations on the aforementioned lapses and submit the original copy of the distribution lists duly certified by the barangay captain as presented in the annex attached to the AOM issued to management, otherwise the transactions will be suspended or disallowed in audit;
instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that for transactions of the same nature, the original copy of the distribution list of welfare goods or any items distributed to constituents be certified by the head of office making the distribution and the same must be attached to the disbursement voucher to be submitted to COA, and
require the Municipal Social Welfare Officer or the concerned head of office, the  Supply Officer and the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the   procurement for welfare goods or any items for distribution  are fully accounted for by  preparing the list before the distribution,  indicating among others the  title of the activity such as “Distribution of Welfare Goods for Typhoon Mario victims”, the date of distribution, columns for the name of the recipient, address of the recipient,  quantity and complete specifications of the items to be distributed and recipient’s signatures.

We recommended that Management prioritize the creation of the LGU’s official website.  While the website is not yet operational, the Municipal Mayor may consider submitting a written notice for the Speaker of the House of Representatives, President of the Senate of the Philippines and the Chairpersons of the House Committee on Appropriations and Senate Committee on Finance about the non-posting of the Reports on the Utilization and Status of PPA Implementation of the BGCM in the website and the reason therefor.  Once the agency website is operational, require the  Local Finance Committee to post the required reports at the LGU’s website in order to promote transparency and easier access to information, enabling anybody to monitor and evaluate the LGU’s response and measures to combat the COVID-19 emergency.

We recommended that henceforth, Management ensure the preparation of the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and submit the same to NDRRMC, through the OCD and copy furnished the COA Auditor within ten (10) days after the end of each quarter to enable proper monitoring and evaluation of donations received by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to obligate the expenditures at the gross amount and fill out all the data indicated in the CAFOA in order to ascertain that the expenditures being obligated are within the budget and are charged to the proper office, function/program/project and account code  to avoid the incurrence of overdraft in appropriation and facilitate easy verification of the Statement of Comparison of Budget versus Actual Amounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary documents for the application of bond for the three accountable officers and the payment of the corresponding bond premiums in order to protect the interest of the government.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor appoint permanent Municipal Assessor and MCR for a more efficient delivery of services to the constituents.

We recommended that henceforth, the municipality avail the insurance coverage of members of the Barangay Tanod and the Municipal Rescue Team from the GSIS which, under the law, administers the insurance coverage of government officials and employees.

We recommended that Management fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan by facilitating compliance with the DENR requirements for the operation of the landfill; speeding up the completion of the second cell; imposing stricter implementation of waste segregation at source, and conducting information dissemination/imposing fines on burning.
We also recommended that in close coordination with the barangays, Management adopt a more stringent approach for the implementation of the pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 in order to safeguard public health from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to stop the practice of splitting contracts just to avoid public bidding. Require the BAC to conduct competitive bidding for all procurements and resort to alternative methods of procurement only when conditions provided in the 2016 IRR of RA 9184 are met. Moreover, the BAC should see to it that the most advantageous price is obtained in every procurement.

We recommended that the BAC require the BAC Secretariat to post the Notices of Award in the PhilGEPS within three (3) calendar days from its issuance as prescribed in Section 37.1.6 of 2016 IRR of RA 9184 to promote transparency in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management instruct the BAC Secretariat to always check the amount and date of the official receipt presented by the bidders before releasing the bidding documents or accepting the bid proposals. Further, require the collecting officer to secure a copy of the Invitation to Bid for every project from the BAC Secretariat to have a reference as to the cost of bidding documents to be collected and see to it that the amount collected is counterchecked in the table of standard rates provided in Section 5 of Appendix 8 of the 2016 IRR of RA 9184.

We reiterate our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to see to it that computation of monthly payrolls includes deductions from the salaries of the concerned municipal officials and employees the remaining under-withheld taxes during 2019 aggregating P953,384.67 and remit the same to BIR within the reglementary period in order help the national government to raise funds for its various programs and projects.  Further, we recommend that the interest and penalties in connection with the late remittance of taxes shall not be charged against funds of the municipality but rather a personal liability of the Municipal Accountant who was in charge in the withholding of correct amount of tax due.

We recommended that Management inform the Auditor in writing on the posting of infrastructure projects billboards within ten days after the award of the contract and comply with the submission of monthly report of on-going and completed contracts as required under COA Circular 2013-004 dated January 20, 2013 in order to promote transparency in the implementation of infrastructure projects and to ensure that the right of the people to information on matters of public concern is addressed.

We recommended that henceforth, management dispense with providing meals to RHU personnel during the relaxed quarantine period in order to save funds for more meaningful COVID related expenditures such as giving additional assistance to the less advantaged constituents.

We recommended that:
a). 	 the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the SB adopt and implement guidelines as regards the granting of communication expenses taking into consideration the limits on the amounts to be claimed as well as the personnel who are entitled for such allowance;
b).  	the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that all payments for cell cards  are supported with list of recipients indicating among others the quantity and complete specifications of items distributed which must be duly acknowledged by the end users  and a certification by the agency head or his authorized representative that such cell cards were used for official purposes only,
c). 	 Management consider the availment of postpaid plan offered by telecommunication networks for elected officials and heads of offices, and that any amount in excess of the plan shall be charged against the personal account of the official/employee concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special account under the General Fund for the operation of the public market and prepare the required records and reports in order to provide all interested parties ready information regarding the performance and profitability of this venture and serves as guide for management in monitoring operational efficiency and realization of income instead of incurring net loss.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that all disbursements are supported with valid and complete documents. Further, management should adhere to Section 9.4 of the RRSA regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances. Audit suspensions should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances

Direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer, in the absence of the Supply and Property Custodian, in ensuring that:
The perpetual inventory method is observed in the accounting of inventories;
The required forms, subsidiary records and periodic reports are prepared and maintained for the recognition of expense portion of the supplies inventories; and
Periodic physical count of inventory on hand is conducted to pave way for any necessary adjustments in the books and/or inventory records for proper valuation.
Require the supplier to stop issuing official receipt if payment is not yet made. Instead, issue charge invoice and statement of account to inform the Municipality of its obligation.
We recommended that Management comply with the posting requirements provided under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 on the projects undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act.

Submit justification/s for the failure of the LGU to comply with the documents required as stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement signed between the Municipality and DSWD on April 23, 2020.
Moving forward, ensure strict compliance with the roles and responsibilities required for the LGU in accordance with the stipulations set forth in the Memorandum of Agreement signed between the Municipality and other agencies.

Immediately stop the payment of honoraria to COVID19 front liners which did not fall within the guidelines set under DBM Budget Circular No. 2007-2 dated October 1, 2007 and provide a valid justifications on the LGU’s granting of honorarium instead of hazard pay to qualified personnel as COVID19 front liners; and
For future transactions, attach a documentary checklist per disbursement voucher to ensure that every transaction has complete supporting documents in compliance with Section 4(6) of PD 1445 and Section 1.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.

Provide depreciation charges to the PPE additions during the year as well as to the PPEs already counted physically and actually recorded in the books of accounts; and
For unreconciled PPE accounts, prioritize the conduct of inventory and reconciliation and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect their correct balances by providing depreciation charges to depreciable assets, request write-off and disposal for unserviceable properties.

Immediately submit the approved DV together with the lacking supporting documents and submit the approved contract to COA for review; and
Moving forward, direct the requesting office/unit to attach in the face of the DV the documentary checklist per COA Circular 2012-001 according to their nature of transactions and for the Accountant to check to ensure completeness of supporting documents before payment.
Stop the practice of indicating brand names in the Purchase Request and adhere to the provision of Section 18 of RA 9184.

Submit the lacking documents as enumerated in Section 13 of COA  Circular No. 2012-001 in support of the road right of way expenditures; and
For future transactions, attach a documentary checklist per disbursement voucher to ensure that said transaction has complete supporting documents.
Strictly comply with the provisions under Section 4 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-01 dated June 14, 2012 and submit the lacking supporting documents for reference in audit.

Require the management utmost diligence in the performance of their duties and responsibilities concerning disbursement of government funds and observe proper internal control in accordance with PD 1445.
Moving forward, consistently use the new format of DV and CAFOA as prescribed under GAM across all Offices in the LGU; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to only issue checks with complete signatories in the disbursement vouchers and signed by the payee as proof of receipt of payment.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly comply with the provisions of Department of Interior and Local Government and Department of Budget and Management Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017, that charges to 20% Development Fund shall be for capital expenditure projects which contribute to the socio-economic development and environment management to preclude disallowances in an audit and expenses which are administrative in nature should be charged against the General Fund.

We reiterated the following recommendations:

Direct the Santiago City Inventory and Disposal Committee to (i) conduct a complete physical count annually as required; (ii) intensify efforts to reconcile the result of physical count with property and accounting records and (iii) recommend for the adjustment of any noted discrepancy in compliance with Paragraph 2 of Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs;

Direct the concerned City Officials to maintain and regularly update property cards, subsidiary ledgers, property ledger cards, stock cards and other subsidiary records in conformity with Section 13, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs; and 

Derecognize unserviceable properties from the books, stop providing depreciation charges for these assets and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: .  The Inventory Committee conducted partial inventory of existing assets.  Inventory was limited to property records from 2013 to present because majority of old records are not available.  Reconciliation with the records of the City Accounting Office is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed;  to reconcile said report with the records of the City Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.

Direct the City Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories as prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to:
a. require the Municipal Engineer to prepare an updated and corrected Report
on Public Infrastructures and furnish a copy of said report to the Property and
Accounting Offices;
b. require the Inventory Committee to include Public Infrastructures and
Parks, Plaza and Monuments in the conduct of physical count of PPE;
c. require the Municipal Accountant to prepare adjusting entries to drop
from the books of accounts the unserviceable and donated properties;
d. require the Supply Officer to make proper disposal of unserviceable
properties;
e. require the Supply Officer to maintain complete, accurate and updated
Property Cards (PCs) based on records and reconcile the same regularly
with the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELCs)
maintained by the Accounting Office;
f. require the Supply Officer to maintain a copy of every PAR and cause the
renewal of the same every three years or whenever there is a change in
custodianship;
g. require the Property Unit and the Accounting Office to effect the necessary
adjustments in their records, if any, in order to reflect the correct balance of
every PPE account, and
h. require the Supply Officer to submit duly accomplished RPCPPE to the
Auditor not later than January 31 of the ensuing year.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a) Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and
the GSO with the complete description and cost segregation of road
components for road projects;
b) Require the inventory committee to conduct physical count of all its local
road network system and prepare the Report on the physical Count of
Road Network System;
c) Require the Supply Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road
Map and keep a complete record for all roads in its component;
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to substantiate with the required
reports and records the amount of P80,417,187.46 Public Infrastructures
which were recorded back to the PPE accounts during the previous years;
and
e) Require the Municipal Accountant to record back to the appropriate PPE
accounts the remaining amount of P118,633,949.23 Public Infrastructures
retrieved from General Journals, substantiate the same with complete
records and reports and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the
Local Road Network System of the municipality.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to consider the
recording in the books of accounts of the proposed adjusting journal entries in
order to reflect the correct balances of affected accounts in the financial
statements. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant
henceforth be guided by the Standard Chart of Accounts in recording
financial transactions in the books of accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting journal entries to reflect the correct balances of the
affected accounts in the financial statements. Henceforth, she should always be
guided by the Chart of Accounts in recording financial transactions in the
books of accounts in order to present fairly the balances of accounts in the
financial statements so as not to mislead users and readers thereof.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare the
necessary adjusting entry to correct the balances of the affected accounts.
Henceforth, she should see to it the financial transactions are recorded in
accordance with the prescribed IPSAS Chart of accounts in order to present
fairly the balances of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to trace the
discrepancy between the balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and
Due to Other Funds and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of
accounts through the preparation of Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) duly
supported with documents, explanations and/or computations in order to
reflect the correct balances of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that henceforth, the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Accountant to record in the agency books all procurement of
supplies for stock as Inventories and issuances thereof to the appropriate
expense accounts duly supported with Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and
Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI) in order to reflect the
correct balances of affected accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to furnish the Auditor a copy
of all contracts together with the required supporting documents within five
(5) working days after execution in order for the Audit Team to perform
timely review of the same and conduct inspection of the completed projects so
that deficiencies could immediately be detected, communicated and corrected
and sanctions for unjustified failure to comply will be accordingly avoided.

We recommended and Management agreed to inform the Auditor in
writing on the posting of infrastructure projects billboards within ten (10)
days after the award of the contract and comply with the submission of
monthly report of on-going and completed contracts as required under COA
Circular 2013-004 dated January 20, 2013.

We recommended that Management henceforth ensure that all revenue
and disbursement documents including the monthly bank reconciliation
statements shall be submitted to the Commission on Audit not later than the
10th day of the following month and the quarterly trial balances be submitted
until the 20th day after the end of each quarter. Moreover, it is strongly
recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause for the suspension of the
payment of salary of the Municipal Accountant if she fails to submit the
required reports and records on time as provided under Section 122 (2) of PD
1445.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare
immediately the disbursement vouchers for the remittance of the trust
collections due to the barangays and the Provincial Government and the
Municipal Treasurer to prepare the corresponding checks for the remittance.
Henceforth, all trust collections due to other government units should be
remitted promptly in order for them to use the much needed funds for their
operations and to avoid misappropriation/misuse of the funds.

We reiterated our recommendation that management see to it that the
use of government vehicles should be supported with properly accomplished
and duly approved Driver’s Trip Ticket which shall be serially numbered, a
summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in the Monthly
Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels. A copy
of these reports should be submitted monthly to the Audit Team as required in
COA Circular No. 77-61.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause for the immediate
remittance of the tax deficiencies aggregating P1,522,477.90 in order to avoid
further interests and penalties, anyway, the amount will redound to the benefit
of the municipality in the form of Internal Revenue Allotments (IRA).
Further, we recommended that the interest and penalties in connection with
the late remittance of taxes shall not be charged against funds of the
municipality but rather a personal liability of the Municipal Accountant who
was in charge in the withholding and remittance of correct amount of tax due.

We recommended and management agreed to avoid paying overtime
services rendered in excess of the regular working hours without considering
the possibility of CTO as prescribed under CSC and DBM Joint Circular No.
1, s. 2015. Furthermore, we recommended that Management stop the
practice of charging expenditures not covered by appropriation otherwise the
same shall be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that management require the concerned employees to
prepare and promptly submit liquidations for each cash advance when the
purpose for the grant has been served. Further, we recommended that the
Accounting Office closely monitor the outstanding balances by informing the
accountable officers in writing to settle their cash advances and require the
liquidation of all cash advances at the end of the year except for petty cash
fund.

We recommended and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC)
Chairman agreed to follow strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised
Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to attain competitive
bidding and to guarantee that the programs and projects in the municipality
may not be put at risk during the implementation of the infrastructure
projects and the delivery of goods.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LDRRMO to
prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund and
submit to the COA Auditor not later than the 15th day after the end of each
month. We recommended further that personnel assigned to LDRRM Office
keep themselves updated on reporting requirements and subsequent issuances
to purposively gather relevant data and periodically summarize the same, not
only for compliance but most importantly for decision-making purposes.

We recommended that Management fully implement its Solid Waste
Management Plan by facilitating compliance with the DENR requirements for
the operation of the landfill; speeding up the construction of access road;
imposing stricter implementation of waste segregation at source; conducting
information dissemination/imposing fines on burning; and ensuring the
procurement of garbage compactor.
We also recommended that in close coordination with the barangays,
Management adopt a more stringent approach for the implementation of the
pertinent provisions of Republic Act No. 9003 in order to safeguard public
health from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a
safe, clean and green environment.

We recommended and Management agreed that the:
GAD Focal Point System -
 intensify and monitor the implementation of planned GAD activities in
order to address broader scope of gender issues.
Management -
 create a GAD Monitoring and Evaluation (M&E) Team who shall
evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, PPAs in accordance
with item 5.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC 2013-01. The M&E
activities shall be included in the annual GPB; and
 see to it that the annual GAD Plan and Budget and Accomplishment
Report be furnished to COA as prescribed under COA Circular No.
2014-001.

We recommended that henceforth, Management consider the Return on
Investment (ROI) or the costs of the assets plus reasonable amount of profit
for renting out such facilities in determining the minimum rental fees for
Investment Properties so as to generate income which is the very purpose of
these investments and not to incur loss to the detriment of the government.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain special account under the General Fund for the
operation of the public market and prepare the required records and reports
in order to provide all interested parties ready information regarding the
performance and profitability of this venture and serves as guide for
management in monitoring operational efficiency and realization of income
instead of incurring net loss. Further, the Municipal Treasurer should collect
the unpaid stall rentals. It is also recommended that management give
priority attention on the cleanliness of the public market in order to attract
more customers.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct all concerned
operating units to facilitate the prompt implementation of all
projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to provide to
the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early
enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.

We recommended that henceforth, management intensify and closely
monitor the implementation of proposed priority programs/projects/activities
under the 20% DF in order to deliver on time the benefits the constituents
could derive therefrom and achieve socio-economic development,
environmental management outcomes and broader scope of development.
Furthermore, in order to avoid delay in the procurement of goods due to
insufficiency of appropriation, it is recommended that Management consider
conducting canvass of prices before the preparation of the approved budget
for the contract which is the basis in the preparation of appropriation
ordinance.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed not to process
claims for payment unless these are completely documented. Likewise, the
Municipal Engineer agreed to submit the lacking documents of infrastructure
projects as required in the Notices of Suspensions. Further, he agreed to see to
it that all infrastructure projects are completed in accordance with plans and
specifications and the computation of the programs of work which is the basis
of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) should be reasonable in order
to avoid disallowance in audit.

We reiterated the following recommendations:
Direct the Santiago City Inventory and Disposal Committee to (i) conduct a complete physical count annually as required; (ii) intensify efforts to reconcile the result of physical count with property and accounting records and (iii) recommend for the adjustment of any noted discrepancy in compliance with Paragraph 2 of Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS for LGUs;
Direct the concerned City Officials to maintain and regularly update property cards, subsidiary ledgers, property ledger cards, stock cards and other subsidiary records in conformity with Section 13, Volume II of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Derecognize unserviceable properties from the books, stop providing depreciation charges for these assets and facilitate disposal of these properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that Management:
Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed;  to reconcile said report with the records of the City Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semesters, respectively.
Direct the City Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories as prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Instruct the City General Services Officer (GSO) and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the City Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.
3.4	We recommended for the immediate return of the unused balances of completed projects to the source agencies to comply with Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the LDRRM Officer to submit annually a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) compliant to RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 11, 2002 containing a complete functional classification, list of specific projects and activities with sufficient details of funding whether current year appropriation, continuing appropriation or special trust funds from unexpended DRRMF of prior years, copy furnish the COA Audit Team.  Likewise, we recommended that the City Mayor requires the LDDRM Officer to conform with the prescribed format of the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.

5.9 We recommended that the Management ensure strict adherence to the laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advance, and:
Enforce the immediate liquidation and/or settlement of all outstanding cash advances. Cause the withholding of the salaries of AOs who could not submit liquidation documents without valid reasons and impose the administrative sanction, if warranted;
Stop the practice of granting additional cash advances to the same officials/employees who have previous unliquidated cash advances regardless of the purpose; and
Trace the records of old accounts and maintain a complete subsidiary records.

We recommended that the City Government of Santiago undertake a collective effort to revise the Schedule of Fair Market Values (SFMV) of real properties to update the real property tax assessments in compliance with Section 219 of RA 7160.

7.6 We recommended that Management require the concerned officials of the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to submit Infrastructure Contracts and Purchase Orders within five (5) days after its perfection duly supported with all the supporting documents required under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009 and a transmittal letter containing a list of supporting documents attached to the advance copy of infrastructure contract/purchase order.
8.5 We recommended that the GSO be required to submit the Acceptance and Inspection Reports (AIR) to the Office of the Auditor within twenty four (24) hours from acceptance of procured goods.

9.4 We recommended that the City Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee, the City Health Office and other concerned officials to adhere with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 to ensure the safety and quality of procured drugs and medicines.

We recommended and management agreed that in the course of the conduct of inventory of PPE in 2020, the inventory committee will completely identify insurable properties to be covered by insurance in order for the City to fully comply with Section 5 of the “Property Insurance Law”.

We reiterated our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management should meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Program and promptly implement programs/projects/activities embodied in the 20 percent LDF in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG & DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated 22 February 2017.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary records of all fund transfers from National Government Agencies.   Likewise, direct all concerned personnel who are directly in-charge of the implementation of projects to regularly submit the required reports and complete supporting documents to the City Accountant and the Office of the Auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management make representations with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to facilitate the enactment of the Market Code and renewal of lease contracts of market stallholders to maximize the revenue earning capacity of the City.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the SCGADC and all the departments shall submit regularly GAD Accomplishment Reports with the corresponding actual costs for particular programs/projects for review and evaluation of accomplishments.

1.10   We recommended and Management agreed to create an inventory team and exert more effort in the conduct and completion of the RPCPPE to ensure that the PPE balances reflected in the FS are fairly presented.

hasten the updating of property records in order to maintain    accurate records of the Municipality’s properties.

6.6 We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the BAC   Secretariat to ensure that the Notice of Award, Notice to proceed and Approved Contract for the procurements undertaken by the LGU are posted in the PhilGEPS.

8.5    We recommended and Management agreed to:
adjust the charging of ₱49,500.00 from LDRRMF to the proper program which supports the programs and activities of the Philippine National Police.
prohibit the charging of expenses which are not related to disaster risk management activities to the LDRRMF.

10.7 We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure allowable expenses are charged to 20% Development Fund in compliance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed;  to reconcile said report with the records of the City Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.

Direct the City Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories as prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and


Instruct the City General Services Officer (GSO) and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the City Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.
observations.reason: Partial Implementation due to lack of manpower and incomplete records.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that Management require the Accounting Department to:
Reconcile their records of Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees account to arrive at a correct/reconciled balances and draw the necessary adjusting entries on the errors committed promptly in compliance with Section 10, Volume II, of the MNGAS for LGUs; and


Analyze and ascertain the validity of long outstanding payables.  Those found valid should be paid immediately.  On the other hand, recorded payables without valid documentation and which have been outstanding for two or more years and against which no actual claim had been filed should be, with prior concurrence of the Sangguniang Panlungsod, reverted to Government Equity, to clear payable accounts, as prescribed under Section 98 of PD No. 1445.  Henceforth, only obligations with proper and complete documentation should be recorded in the books of accounts.
