Henceforth, furnish the Auditor a copy of all contracts and purchase orders together with the required supporting documents within five (5) working days after execution of the contract/purchase order in order for the Audit Team to perform timely review of the same and conduct inspection of the completed projects/items delivered so that deficiencies could immediately be detected and corrected.

We recommended that management see to it that the use of government vehicles should be supported with properly accomplished and duly approved Driver’s Trip Ticket which shall be serially numbered, a summary of which shall be made at the end of the month in the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels.  A copy of these reports should be submitted monthly to the Audit Team as required in COA Circular No.  77-61.   Moreover, a certified list of Municipal Vehicles should also be furnished to the Audit Team for verification.

In order to correct the aforementioned deficiencies, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a).  require the Municipal Assessor Designate to prepare and submit to the Municipal Treasurer  on or before the 31st of December of each year, an assessment roll containing a list of all persons whose real properties have been duly assessed or re-assessed and the value of such properties;
b).  require the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year,  a duly certified list  showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible during the year.
c).  require the Municipal Accountant to draw a JEV to set up the RPT and SET Receivable at the beginning of the year based on the certified list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer in order to reflect the correct amount due and collectible from taxpayers.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare immediately the disbursement vouchers for the remittance of the trust collections due to the Barangay and Provincial Governments.  Henceforth, all trust collections due to other government units should be remitted promptly in order for them to use the  much needed funds for their operations and to avoid misappropriation/misuse of the funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant justify the above lapses. Succeeding lower deduction of withholding tax from the salaries of officials and employees should be effected on time. Likewise, cause for the submission of computation on the tax refunds made to officials and employees which were suspended in audit.

We recommend that the municipality increase the bond premium payment for the Municipal Treasurer commensurate to the maximum cash accountability as prescribed in Annex C of Treasury Circular No. 02-2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that all  data in the JEV should be filled out and that the JEVs are duly supported with complete documents including explanations/computations to   warrant easy verification on the validity, legality and propriety of the transactions including the correctness of  accounting entries made.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer obligate the expenditures at the gross amount and fill up all the data indicated in the OBR in order to ascertain that the expenditures being obligated are within the budget and are charged to the proper office, function/program/project and account code  to avoid the incurrence of overdraft in appropriation and facilitate easy verification of the Statement of Comparison of Budget versus Actual Amounts.

In order to avoid the issuance of suspension orders to contractors and ensure that infrastructure projects are completed on time, we recommend that management:
a).  	conduct detailed engineering investigations including right of way acquisitions before the conduct of bidding and award of contract for infrastructure projects;
b).  	consider the topography of the region, weather condition and prevalence of typhoons thereat in the determination of the contract time and the period of project implementation, and
c). 		issue suspension orders to contractors only when necessary and the notice to resume work should be issued immediately after the reason for the suspension of work has been resolved.
We recommended that the BAC Chairman require the BAC Secretariat to post the Notice of Award in the Philgeps within three (3) calendar days from its issuance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to maintain special account under the General Fund for the operation of the public market and prepare the required records and reports in order to provide all interested parties ready information regarding the performance and profitability of this venture and serves as guide for management in monitoring operational efficiency and realization of income instead incurring net loss.  It is also recommended that management give priority attention on the cleanliness of the public market in order to attract more customers.

We recommended that management enforce the immediate collection of the unpaid stall rentals of P139,000.00.  Consider the revision of the revenue code to include provision of higher rate of monthly stall rental commensurate to the rising cost of electricity and services.  It is also recommended that the amount of monthly rental be indicated in the Contract of Lease and all the provisions therein should be strictly enforced.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco excise taxes in order to give the tobacco farmers who worked hard for the generation of the funds the early enjoyment of benefits they rightfully deserve.

We recommended that the municipal officials and employees concerned implement fully all the programs/ projects / activities embodied in the 20% Development Fund in order to deliver on time the benefits the constituents could derive therefrom.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause the prompt implementation of all funds received from other national government agencies in order to deliver on time the benefits due to the intended beneficiaries and to avoid returning the unutilized funds to the donor agencies.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor require the Chairperson of the GAD Focal Point System to fully implement programs/projects funded under the GAD Plan in order to fully address gender issues/problems of the constituents in the municipal level and to help the national government attain its goal of addressing gender issues in the country.

We recommended that the municipality fully implement its Solid Waste Management Plan and the pertinent provisions of Republic Act. No. 9003 in order to safeguard the public health  from substantial present and potential hazardous wastes and attain a safe, clean and green environment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant not to process claims for payment without complete documentation in order to avoid suspension in audit.  Moreover, the Municipal Engineer should see to it that the actual percentages of accomplishment on infrastructure projects are reflected in the Statement of Work Accomplished and other documents which are the bases for payments to avoid disallowances.  Further, management should adhere to Sections 9.4 and 10.4 of COA Circular 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009 regarding the settlement of suspensions and disallowances.  Audit suspensions should be settled within ninety (90) calendar days from receipt of the NS to avoid maturity of these suspensions to disallowances and disallowances should be settled within six (6) months from receipt of the ND by the persons liable.

We recommended that Management:
Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed;  to reconcile said report with the records of the City Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.
Direct the City Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories as prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Instruct the City General Services Officer (GSO) and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the City Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.

We have recommended that Management require the Accounting Department to:
Reconcile their records of Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees account to arrive at a correct/reconciled balances and draw the necessary adjusting entries on the errors committed promptly in compliance with Section 10, Volume II, of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Analyze and ascertain the validity of long outstanding payables.  Those found valid should be paid immediately.  On the other hand, recorded payables without valid documentation and which have been outstanding for two or more years and against which no actual claim had been filed should be, with prior concurrence of the Sangguniang Panlungsod, reverted to Government Equity, to clear payable accounts, as prescribed under Section 98 of PD No. 1445.  Henceforth, only obligations with proper and complete documentation should be recorded in the books of accounts.
We recommended for the immediate return of the unused balances of completed projects to the source agencies to comply with Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the City of Santiago stop the practice of granting cash advances for purposes beyond the scope prescribed under COA Circular 97-002, strictly observe procurement laws, rules and regulations, and adhere with COA Circular No. 2012-003.

We recommended that the City Accountant facilitate the maintenance of special accounts in the General Fund for economic enterprises and 20% Development Fund as mandated by Section 313 of RA No. 7160 and Section 105, Volume I of NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the LDRRM Officer to submit annually a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) compliant to RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 11, 2002 containing a complete functional classification, list of specific projects and activities with sufficient details of funding whether current year appropriation, continuing appropriation or special trust funds from unexpended DRRMF of prior years, copy furnish the COA Audit Team.  Likewise, we recommended that the City Mayor requires the LDDRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the City Mayor:
Require the City Treasurer’s Office to submit the Reports of Checks Issued and Report of Collections and Deposit daily to the Accounting Office pursuant to Section 55 on the Manual of NGAS and to facilitate submission to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period;
Instruct the City Accountant to record entries in the Journals on a daily basis or as soon as the financial documents are submitted by the City Treasurer’s Office to prevent delay in the preparation of the financial statements; and
Require the City Accountant to submit the financial reports, disbursement vouchers and supporting documents within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct all Revenue Collectors to prepare the RAAF and the City Treasurer to prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

We recommended that the City Government of Santiago cause the increase of the coverage of Fidelity of Accountable Officers exceeding their insurance coverage and strictly observe Treasury Circular No. 2-2009 to properly safeguard the interest of the government.

We recommended and management agreed that in the course of the conduct of inventory of PPE in 2018, the Santiago City Property Inventory and Disposal Committee (SCPIDC) will completely identify insurable properties to be covered by insurance in order for the City to fully comply with Section 5 of the “Property Insurance Law”.

We reiterate our prior years’ audit recommendation that Management meticulously and judiciously review and evaluate projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Program and promptly implement programs/project/activities embodied in the 20 percent LDF in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG & DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated 22 February 2017.

We recommended that Management direct the City Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary records of all fund transfers from National Government Agencies.   Likewise, direct all concerned personnel who are directly in-charge of the implementation of projects to regularly submit the required reports and complete supporting documents to the City Accountant and the Office of the Auditor in compliance with COA Circular No. 94-013.

We recommended that Management make representations with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to facilitate the enactment of the Market Code and renewal of lease contracts of market stallholders to maximize the revenue earning capacity of the City.

We reiterate our previous year’s recommendation that the SCGADC and all the departments shall submit regularly GAD Accomplishment Reports with the corresponding actual costs for particular programs/projects for review and evaluation of accomplishments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive designate a General Services Officer to do the custodial function of the properties.  In the absence of a GSO, the Municipal Treasurer should be the responsible officer for the said function.   Also, maintain property cards for each type of property, plant and equipment.  Moreover, the LCE should create an inventory committee for the conduct of annual physical count of properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the Public Infrastructures with registries for the years under the NGAS.   Also, other concerned offices should coordinate closely in order to assist the Municipal Accountant to comply with COA Circulars 2016-004 and 2015-008 with regard to accounting and reporting on the Local Roads Asset Management System in consistent with the adoption of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the LGU.  Also, the Local Chief Executive should enjoin the department heads’ compliance with the requirements of the circulars.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer make verification of the daily RCDs submitted by the collectors in order to account the completeness of collections made and remitted.  Likewise, collections should be deposited intact and regularly.  This will preclude intentional holding of cash that may result to loss or misuse of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the quarterly trial balance   and monthly bank reconciliation statements together with the supporting documents.  Moreover, refrain from delayed submission of disbursement vouchers and report of collections and deposits in order to have timely audit of the accounts of the Municipality.

We recommended that the approved contracts together with their supporting documents be submitted to the Audit Team for contract review within five (5) working days from issuance as prescribed in Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular 2009-001dated February 12, 2009 in order to have a timely review, evaluation and inspection of the goods delivered and infrastructure projects entered into by the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  submit liquidation reports of those implemented projects under the Bottom-up Budgeting program and Core Local Access Road and refund those unutilized balance in order to reflect in the books of both the transferor (DILG)  and transferee (LGU-Santiago) the full liquidation of the funds.

We recommended that the municipality insure other properties such as the rural health unit building and public market to GSIS in order to ensure that the LGU will be indemnified or compensated in case those properties will suffer any damage or loss due to fire, earthquake, storm, or other fortuitous event.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) follow strictly the provisions of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 in order to attain competitive bidding and to guarantee that the programs and projects in the municipality may not be at risks during the implementation of infrastructure projects and the delivery of goods.

We recommended that the BAC-Technical Working Group (TWG) conduct validation on the Statement of On-going Government and Private Contracts and on their Net Financial Contracting Capacity (NFCC) during the Post Qualification in order to attain the Lowest Calculated Responsive Bid.  Moreover, the members of the BAC should be aware on their issuance of BAC Resolutions which should be based on the actual sequential proceedings.

We recommended that goods procured through shopping should be supported with BAC Resolution Adopting the Alternative Mode of Procurement through Shopping.  Moreover, refrain from splitting of contracts.  Goods procured under the LDRRM fund should be properly planned in order that procurement will be in bulk to avail competitive price.  Also, goods procured with material amount and do not fall in emergency cases should be paid in check to suppliers with complete supporting documents.
We recommended that the municipality require a warranty from the contract awardees or suppliers for the procurement of goods in order to assure that manufacturing defects shall be corrected.

We recommended that those projects, activities and programs in the LDRRM Investment Plan that were stated in generic terms be supported with approved budgetary requirements so that specific expenditures chargeable to each PAP will be evaluated and implemented effectively and efficiently in order that the fund will be suited to the local needs for prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation and recovery.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Engineer   submit the approved plans and drawings, program of works, statement of work accomplished and inspection report of the Agency Project Monitoring Committee in order to have a basis in the inspection of the completed infrastructure projects implemented by administration.

We recommended that the Local School Board refrain from charging expenditure which is not among the allowable expenses enumerated in Section 4 of DepEd-DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 2017.
On the other hand, in response with the management’s comments on the above observation, expenditures on volunteer teachers should be properly documented as to their authority to render their services with proof of compliance with the said joint circular.  While attendance to competitions which are considered DepEd related under the Sports Development should be properly identified by DepEd through circular or memorandum and for the absence of DepED funding in the implementation of Alternative Learning System (ALS), it should be supported with certification from authorized official in the division office.
Based on the foregoing, due to the absence of these necessary documents to support the said expenditures, these were not allowed charges under SEF.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local School Board to convene and prepare a comprehensive and detailed budget that supplement the actual operation and maintenance of public schools as mandated in Section 100 (c) of RA 7160.  Each school recipient should submit a request for funding for the review and approval of the board in compliance with the DepEd-DBM DILG   Joint Circular No. 01, s.2017.

We recommended that the municipality through the leadership of the Local Chief Executive monitor the full implementation of the programs/projects appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to guarantee the optimal utilization of the fund in compliance with Section 5 of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.  Also, refrain from charging expenses which are administrative in nature and not related to specified program in compliance with Section 4 of the said circular.  Moreover, see to it that the 20% Development Fund should only be utilized for development projects in the form of investment or capital expenditures.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant  check the completeness of the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers on payment of infrastructure projects.  On the other hand, the Municipal Engineer should submit the approved plans and drawings in order to have a basis in the conduct on inspection.   He should also submit pictures of the projects before, during and after implementation to warrant the existence of the infrastructures implemented.
We recommended that the municipality attach the Purchase of Fertilizers with pre-listing of qualified beneficiaries and properly accomplished list of recipients duly signed.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer require contractors to post tarpaulin signboards at the project site for infrastructure projects to promote transparency and accountability in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2013-004 dated January 30, 2013.  Also, the costs of the signboards included as pay item in the bill of quantities submitted by the contractors amounting to P23,320.00 should be refunded.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers on payment of infrastructure projects.  On the other hand, the Municipal Engineer should submit the approved plans and drawings in order to have a basis in the conduct on inspection. We recommended that the Municipal Government discontinue their practice of settling the suspensions beyond the ninety (90) calendar days from receipt.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the preparation of disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

Create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical count of inventory items as of June 30 and December 31 of each year; to prepare the Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RCPI) by inventory account name and distribute the same as prescribed;  to reconcile said report with the records of the City Accounting Office in accordance with Section 124, Volume I of MNGAS; and to submit to the Office of the Auditor the duplicate copy of the RCPI not later than July 31 and January 31 of each year for the first and second semester, respectively.
Direct the City Accountant to correct the errors in recording purchases and issuances of inventories and observe the Perpetual Inventory System as method of recording inventories as prescribed under Section 51, Volume I of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Instruct the City General Services Officer (GSO) and the City Accountant to maintain updated Stock Cards and Supplies Ledger Cards and regularly reconcile records to detect discrepancies and effect immediate corrections; and for the GSO to promptly prepare and submit the monthly RSMIs to the City Accountant for recording in compliance with Sections 119 and 120, Volume I, of the MNGAS for LGUs.

Reconcile their records of Accounts Payable and Due to Officers and Employees account to arrive at a correct/reconciled balances and draw the necessary adjusting entries on the errors committed promptly in compliance with Section 10, Volume II, of the MNGAS for LGUs; and
Analyze and ascertain the validity of long outstanding payables.  Those found valid should be paid immediately.  On the other hand, recorded payables without valid documentation and which have been outstanding for two or more years and against which no actual claim had been filed should be, with prior concurrence of the Sangguniang Panglungsod, reverted to Government Equity, to clear payable accounts, as prescribed under Section 98 of PD No. 1445.  Henceforth, only obligations with proper and complete documentation should be recorded in the books of accounts.
We recommended for the immediate return of the unused balances of completed projects to the source agencies to comply with Sections 4.9 and 6.7 of COA Circular 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended that the City Mayor require the LDRRM Officer to submit annually a comprehensive Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) compliant to RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 11, 2002 containing a complete functional classification, list of specific projects and activities with sufficient details of funding whether current year appropriation, continuing appropriation or special trust funds from unexpended DRRMF of prior years, copy furnish the COA Audit Team.  Likewise, we recommended that the City Mayor requires the LDDRM Officer to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF within 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that City Mayor requires all Revenue Collectors prepare the RAAF and the City Treasurer prepare the CRAAF and submit the monthly reports of accountability for accountable forms in the prescribed form provided in the Manual for NGAS for LGUs and submit the same not later than the 5th day of the ensuing month to the COA Audit Team.

We recommended and management agreed that in the course of the conduct of inventory of PPE in 2018, the Santiago City Property Inventory and Disposal Committee (SCPIDC) will completely identify insurable properties to be covered by insurance in order for the City to fully comply with Section 5 of the “Property Insurance Law”.

We recommended that Management meticulously and judiciously consider projects before its inclusion in the Local Development Fund and Annual Investment Program and promptly implement programs/project/activities embodied in the 20 per cent LDF in order to achieve the desirable socio-economic and environmental benefits as prescribed under DILG & DBM Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2017-1 dated 22 February 2017.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the City Treasurer’s Office to exert more effort in the collection of taxes due and collectible by sending Notice of Delinquency to RPT Taxpayers and form a committee to conduct an information dissemination regarding the importance of paying taxes promptly and the consequences of non-payment of taxes.  We also recommended imposing the provisions of Sections 258 and 260 of the Local Government Code in the collections of delinquent taxes.

We recommended that Management direct the City School Board to appropriate reasonable amounts from the SEF for the implementation of projects, programs and activities as prescribed under Section 272 of RA 7160 for the improvement and development of public schools and its students in the City of Santiago.
We recommended that Management direct the City School Board to include the unexpended balance of SEF funds in prior years and to submit realistic estimates of SEF receipts to maximize the benefits for SEF beneficiaries in furtherance of education related programs, projects and activities.

We have recommended and Management agreed that the SCGADC and all the departments submit regularly GAD Accomplishment Reports with the corresponding actual costs for particular programs/projects for review and evaluation of accomplishments.

We recommended and Management agreed to comply strictly with the provision of Section 124 of the NGAS in the conduct of physical count and in the submission of the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) and Report of the Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI).

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive designate a General Services Officer to do the custodial function of the properties.  In the absence of a GSO, the Municipal Treasurer should be the responsible officer for the said function.   Also, maintain property cards for each type of property, plant and equipment.  Moreover, the LCE should create an inventory committee for the conduct of annual physical count of properties.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant support the Public Infrastructures with registries for the years under the NGAS.   Also, other concerned offices should coordinate closely in order to assist the Municipal Accountant to comply with COA Circulars 2016-004 and 2015-008 with regard to accounting and reporting on the Local Roads Asset Management System in consistent with the adoption of the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS) in the LGU.  Also, the Local Chief Executive should enjoin the department heads’ compliance with the requirements of the circulars.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the quarterly trial balance and monthly bank reconciliation statements together with the supporting documents within the prescribed time.  Nevertheless, refrain from delayed submission of disbursement vouchers and report of collections and deposits in order to have timely audit of the accounts of the Municipality.

We have recommended that the municipality disburse funds  only  within the approved appropriations for a specific program in order to prevent unrelated expenditures charged to other program.  In addition, support the issuance and distribution of relief goods to end users or beneficiaries with Requisition and Issue Slips properly acknowledged.  Moreover, refrain from procurement of supplies and equipment through reimbursement and cash advance in order to comply the documentary requirements under the procurement law and to attain transparency and reasonable costs of procured goods.   Also, disbursements out of Quick Response Fund should be authorized by a Sanggunian Bayan Resolution.

We have recommended that the Municipal Engineer submit the deficiencies noted during the conduct of inspection of the projects.   For the Embankment at  San Roque, Santiago, Ilocos Sur, submit pictures before, during and after completion of the project.  On the other hand, for the Concreting/Rehabilitation of Farm to Market Road at Gabao, Santiago, Ilocos Sur,  submit pictures as proof of implementation and demolition of the project.  Also, submit certification from the DPWH Engineering Office specifying that there was an existing concrete road which was newly constructed by the LGU-Santiago which was later on demolished   (indicate the actual date of demolition).

We have recommended that the municipal officials and employees refrain from charging fuel consumption through reimbursement.  However, in extreme emergency cases, reimbursement shall be allowed for those authorized officials and employees only with properly attached supporting documents such as gas slip, driver’s trip ticket, travel order and itinerary of travel.  Likewise, the accounting section should properly compute the total amount due on fuel consumed which was availed on credit to supplier/s in order to avoid discrepancies that may result to over/under payment of expenditures.  Moreover, prepare the schedule of sales invoices that will facilitate the computation.

We have recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees  attach the necessary documents showing the purpose and proof of actual utilization of meals and snacks such as a copy of program or other office activities, budget estimates and attendees.    Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should monitor the proper charging of the said expenditures in order not to abuse the government funds.

We have recommended that the concerned department heads and officials submit a utilization report showing the quantities of cell cards being received, used and duly acknowledged by them.    Also,   support the expenditures of cell cards by a Sangguniang Bayan Resolution indicating therein the reasonable amount of cell cards for each department head including the officials of the municipality.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant check the completeness of the supporting documents of the disbursement vouchers on the payment of infrastructure projects and be guided with the provisions of RA 9184.  Likewise, it is recommended that the BAC Secretariat submit the required bidding documents while the Municipal Engineer should submit the program of works, plans and drawings and pictures of the projects in order to verify the reported accomplishments of the completed projects.

We  have recommended that the municipality through the leadership of the Local Chief Executive monitor the full implementation of the programs/projects appropriated under the 20% Development Fund in order to guarantee the optimal utilization of the fund in compliance with Section 5 of the  DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.  Furthermore, those programs and projects described in generic terms should be covered by a sanggunian approval for every specific project to be undertaken.  Also, refrain from charging expenses which are administrative in nature.  Moreover, see to it that the 20% Development Fund should only be utilized for development projects in the form of investment or capital expenditures.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require a project proposal in every undertaking of the LGU that will clearly define the program/project for an effective and cost-efficient delivery of services to its constituents.  Also, the Sanggunian Bayan should see to it that those priority projects submitted by the LCE for appropriations be supported with approved project proposals whenever applicable.

We have recommended that the agency GAD Focal Point person coordinate the preparation of the agency GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) with concerned offices or departments in order to implement all programs and GAD activities identified that will fully address the gender issues for the protection and fulfilment of the rights of women at the socio-cultural, economic and political spheres.

We have recommended that the Municipal Government discontinue their practice of settling the suspensions beyond the ninety (90) calendar days from receipt.  Moreover, the Municipal Accountant should be guided with COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012 in the preparation of disbursement vouchers with the supporting documents to avoid suspensions and disallowances.

1.5 	We recommended that the Management exert more effort towards full settlement of remaining cash advances specifically those that have been outstanding for over one year and more. Continue the strict implementation of the rules on cash advances, issuance of demand letters and the withholding of salaries of the City Officials and employees concerned.

We recommended that Management: (a) Include the reconciliation of accounting records and property records in the duties and functions of the Inventory Team so that the reconciliation of property and accounting records will be performed promptly and effectively; (b) Require the GSO and Accounting department to maintain subsidiary ledgers and  property ledger card for each PPE item and indicate the condition, its whereabouts and other details that are necessary to prove its existence and ownership to serve as the basis for the preparation of the Inventory Report and for easier reconciliation.

5.8 	We recommended that Management require the barangay to submit relevant documents such as feasibility study to ensure that the funds for the proposed program will have a return on its investment and be a source of livelihood to the community.

6.6 	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive cause the full settlement of disallowed payments of P2,484,961.87 and take appropriate steps in requiring strict settlement of accounts from concerned City officials and employees within the prescribed period stated in the regulation.
